```text
0	# Inventorize
1	
2	## App Identity
3	Name:           Inventorize
4	Tagline:        Track your inventory, anytime, anywhere.
5	Industry:       SaaS / internal operations / inventory management
6	Primary users:  Platform super admin, tenant admins, warehouse staff, purchasing staff
7	
8	## Problem Statement
9	Small and mid-size companies still track inventory in spreadsheets — leading to stock discrepancies, no audit trail, and zero visibility into stock movements. Existing inventory software is either too expensive, too complex, or requires on-premise installation. Inventorize provides a multi-tenant SaaS where each company gets its own isolated inventory workspace with barcode scanning, serial tracking, purchase orders, stock movement history, and full audit logging — all from a browser. The platform operator manages tenant onboarding, billing readiness, and cross-tenant health monitoring from a super admin dashboard.
10	
11	## Core User Flows
12	
13	1. **Super admin onboards a new tenant**: Super admin opens tenant management → enters the tenant name → system auto-generates a URL slug (lowercased, spaces and special characters replaced with hyphens). The slug field is shown pre-filled and editable before saving. A real-time uniqueness check runs as the super_admin types — if the slug is already taken, an inline error is shown ("This slug is already taken — please choose another") and save is blocked. A URL preview is shown beneath the slug field (e.g. inventorize.powerbyte.app/[slug]/dashboard). Super admin also enters the contact email → system provisions tenant workspace with subdirectory route (app.com/tenant-slug/) → super admin creates the tenant's first admin user account with tenant assignment → system generates a one-time account setup token (cryptographically random, stored as a hash in the database, 24-hour expiry, single-use) → tenant admin receives welcome email containing a link to /auth/setup?token=[token] → admin clicks link, is prompted to set their own password, and logs in normally. The token is invalidated immediately on use or after 24 hours, whichever comes first. If the link expires, the super_admin can trigger a password reset from the user management page. Once the tenant is created, the slug is permanent and cannot be changed. Error: user creation fails → roll back tenant record, show retry option.
14	
15	2. **Super admin manages the platform**: Super admin views all tenants list with status (active/suspended/trial) → can suspend or reactivate a tenant → can view cross-tenant metrics (total tenants, total products across platform, active users per tenant) → can impersonate a tenant admin for support (read-only mode) → all super admin actions create AuditLog entries. Impersonation is strictly read-only: server-side tRPC middleware returns FORBIDDEN on all mutation procedures when session.isImpersonating = true. Client-side UI also disables all action buttons, form submissions, and destructive controls. A persistent banner is shown at the top of every page: "View Only — Impersonating [Tenant Name]". All page visits during impersonation are written to the platform AuditLog with the impersonated tenantId. Super admin exits impersonation by clicking an "Exit Impersonation" button that clears the flag from the session. Error: suspending a tenant with active users → confirm prompt before proceeding.
16	
17	3. **Tenant admin creates a product**: Tenant admin opens product creation form → enters productCode, barcodeValue, name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled → system auto-calculates sellingPrice → save with currentQuantity = 0 → AuditLog created with tenantId. All data scoped to tenant automatically via RLS. Error: duplicate productCode within tenant → block save. Error: duplicate barcodeValue within tenant → block save.
18	
19	4. **Tenant admin edits a product**: Open existing product → edit allowed fields → recalculate sellingPrice if pricing changed → save → AuditLog with before/after values. Error: invalid values → block save. Note: currentQuantity never editable through this form.
20	
21	5. **Tenant admin or purchasing staff creates a purchase order**: Select supplier (tenant-scoped) → add products with ordered quantities → system snapshots supplierCost → optionally upload attachment → save as "draft" → later update status (ordered → partially_received → received, or cancel) → AuditLog on create and every status change. Error: no items → block. Error: attachment upload fails → preserve PO data, allow retry.
22	
23	6. **Any tenant staff records stock in**: Select product manually or scan product barcode via browser camera → enter quantity → optionally link PO → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/enter serial numbers (count must equal quantity) → optionally upload delivery receipt → system validates → increase Product.currentQuantity atomically → create SerialNumber records as "in_stock" if serial-tracked → create StockMovementLog → AuditLog → if linked to PO, update receivedQty and PO status. Error: duplicate serial for product within tenant → block. Error: any failure → roll back entire transaction.
24	
25	7. **Tenant admin or warehouse staff records stock out**: Select product manually or scan barcode → enter quantity, requestedByName, usedFor, optional notes → if serialTrackingEnabled, scan/select exact serials (must be "in_stock", count must equal quantity) → validate sufficient stock → decrease Product.currentQuantity atomically → update serial status to "issued" → StockMovementLog → AuditLog → optionally generate printable slip. Error: insufficient stock → block, show available. Error: serial not in_stock → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
26	
27	8. **Tenant admin performs stock adjustment**: Enter reason and notes → select product → enter quantityDelta → if serial-tracked, identify serials → validate resulting stock not negative → apply atomically → StockMovementLog → AuditLog. approvedByUserId is optional audit trail only. Error: negative result → block. Error: any failure → roll back.
28	
29	9. **Any tenant user views product history**: Open product detail → view info, current quantity, serials if enabled → view StockMovementLog → filter by date, type, user, reference. Admin sees AuditLog entries. Pricing visibility enforced by role. All data tenant-scoped — users never see other tenants' data.
30	
31	10. **System runs low stock monitoring per tenant**: Daily scheduled job checks products per tenant where currentQuantity <= lowStockThreshold → per-tenant dashboard alert → low stock report → daily email to tenant admin and purchasing_staff → LowStockNotificationLog. Error: email fails → retry 3× with exponential backoff.
32	
33	11. **Any tenant user runs reports and exports**: Open dashboard or reports → apply filters → view data per role permissions → export as CSV → AuditLog for every export. All report data is tenant-scoped.
34	
35	12. **Any tenant user views the tenant dashboard**: User navigates to /[tenant-slug]/dashboard → sees KPI summary cards for the selected period (Today / This Week / This Month / Custom range). The inventory value KPI card is visible to admin and purchasing_staff only — hidden from warehouse_staff. Stock In vs Stock Out bar chart shown for the selected period. Low stock alert banner displayed if any products have currentQuantity <= lowStockThreshold; clicking the banner links to the Low Stock Report. All data is tenant-scoped — no data from other tenants is ever shown.
36	
37	## Modules + Features
38	
39	### Platform Administration (super_admin only)
40	- Tenant list with search, filter by status (active/suspended/trial)
41	- Tenant create form (name, slug [auto-generated from name, editable, real-time uniqueness check, URL preview shown], contact email)
42	- Tenant suspend / reactivate
43	- Cross-tenant metrics dashboard (total tenants, total products, active users per tenant)
44	- Tenant admin impersonation (read-only support mode)
45	- Platform-level audit log viewer
46	
47	### Dashboard (per tenant)
48	- KPI summary cards: total active products, total stock quantity, low stock count, stock in/out counts for period, inventory value (admin/purchasing only)
49	- Stock In vs Stock Out bar chart by period
50	- Low stock alert banner
51	
52	### Products
53	- Product list with search, filter, sort, pagination (tenant-scoped)
54	- Product create/edit form (tenant admin only)
55	- Product detail page with history tab
56	- Barcode scanning for quick product lookup
57	- Serial number list per product (if serial-tracked)
58	
59	### Suppliers
60	- Supplier list with search (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
61	- Supplier create/edit form (tenant-scoped)
62	
63	### Purchase Orders
64	- PO list with status filter (tenant admin and purchasing_staff)
65	- PO create form with line items and attachment upload
66	- PO detail page with status management
67	- PO receiving (links to stock in)
68	
69	### Stock In
70	- Stock in form with barcode scanning
71	- PO linkage (optional)
72	- Serial number entry for serial-tracked products
73	- Delivery receipt upload
74	- Stock in history list
75	
76	### Stock Out
77	- Stock out form with barcode scanning
78	- Serial selection for serial-tracked products
79	- requestedByName and usedFor fields
80	- Printable stock out slip
81	- Stock out history list
82	
83	### Stock Adjustments
84	- Adjustment form (tenant admin only)
85	- Reason and notes required
86	- Positive or negative quantity delta
87	- Serial identification when applicable
88	- Adjustment history list
89	
90	### Reports
91	- Low Stock Report
92	- Stock Movement Report (filterable by date, product, type, user)
93	- Current Inventory Report
94	- Product History Report
95	- Audit Trail Report (tenant admin only, searchable)
96	- CSV export for all reports
97	
98	### Audit Logs
99	- Searchable audit log viewer (tenant admin only — tenant-scoped)
100	- Filter by action type, entity, user, date
101	
102	### Users
103	- User list (tenant admin only — shows only tenant's users)
104	- User create/edit/disable (no hard delete) — managed via Auth.js v5 with sessions in PostgreSQL
105	- Role assignment within tenant
106	
107	## Roles + Permissions
108	
109	| Role | Scope | Can do | Cannot do |
110	|------|-------|--------|-----------|
111	| super_admin | Platform-wide | Manage all tenants (create, suspend, reactivate). View cross-tenant metrics. Impersonate tenant admin (read-only). View platform audit logs. Create first admin user for new tenants. | Cannot modify tenant business data (products, stock, POs). Cannot create stock transactions. Cannot hard-delete tenants. |
112	| admin | Tenant-scoped | Manage users within own tenant (create, update, disable). Manage products (create, edit). View and update all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in/out. Create stock adjustments. View audit logs (own tenant only). View reports and export CSV. | Cannot see other tenants' data. Cannot manage platform. Cannot hard-delete users or products. Cannot directly edit currentQuantity. |
113	| warehouse_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products (selling price only). Create stock in. Create stock out. View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot view supplierCost or markupPercent. Cannot manage users, suppliers, POs, or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
114	| purchasing_staff | Tenant-scoped | View products with all pricing. Manage suppliers. Manage purchase orders. Create stock in. View stock out (read only). View stock movement logs. View reports and export CSV. | Cannot create stock out. Cannot manage users or adjustments. Cannot view audit logs. Cannot see other tenants' data. |
115	
116	## Data Entities
117	
118	### Platform-level (no tenantId)
119	- Tenant: id, name, slug (unique), contactEmail, status (enum: active/suspended/trial), createdAt, updatedAt
120	- User: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for super_admin), name, email, hashedPassword, role (enum: super_admin/admin/warehouse_staff/purchasing_staff), isActive, lastLoginAt, createdAt, updatedAt
121	
122	### Tenant-scoped (all have tenantId, enforced by RLS)
123	- Product: id, tenantId → Tenant, productCode (unique per tenant), barcodeValue (unique per tenant), name, category, unit, supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice (computed), currentQuantity (system-managed), lowStockThreshold, serialTrackingEnabled, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
124	- Supplier: id, tenantId → Tenant, name, contactPerson, phone, email, address, notes, isActive, createdAt, updatedAt
125	- PurchaseOrder: id, tenantId → Tenant, poNumber (unique per tenant), supplierId → Supplier, orderDate, expectedDate, status (enum: draft/ordered/partially_received/received/cancelled), notes, attachmentUrl, createdByUserId → User, createdAt, updatedAt
126	- PurchaseOrderItem: id, purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder, productId → Product, orderedQty, receivedQty, supplierCostSnapshot
127	- StockIn: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), purchaseOrderId → PurchaseOrder (optional), receivedDate, receivedByUserId → User, notes, attachmentUrl, createdAt
128	- StockInItem: id, stockInId → StockIn, productId → Product, quantity, supplierCostSnapshot
129	- StockOut: id, tenantId → Tenant, referenceNumber (unique per tenant), releasedDate, releasedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, notes, printableSlipNumber, createdAt
130	- StockOutItem: id, stockOutId → StockOut, productId → Product, quantity, sellingPriceSnapshot
131	- SerialNumber: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, serialValue, barcodeValue, status (enum: in_stock/issued/adjusted), stockInItemId → StockInItem (optional), stockOutItemId → StockOutItem (optional), createdAt, updatedAt. Unique constraint: [tenantId, productId, serialValue].
132	- StockAdjustment: id, tenantId → Tenant, adjustmentDate, reason, notes, createdByUserId → User, approvedByUserId → User (optional — audit trail only, not blocking), createdAt
133	- StockAdjustmentItem: id, stockAdjustmentId → StockAdjustment, productId → Product, quantityDelta, serialNumberId → SerialNumber (optional)
134	- StockMovementLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, movementType (enum: stock_in/stock_out/adjustment), referenceType, referenceId, quantityBefore, quantityDelta, quantityAfter, serialNumberId (optional), performedByUserId → User, requestedByName, usedFor, performedAt, notes — IMMUTABLE
135	- AuditLog: id, tenantId → Tenant (null for platform-level actions), actorUserId → User, actionType, entityType, entityId, fieldChangesJson, beforeStateJson, afterStateJson, createdAt — IMMUTABLE
136	- LowStockNotificationLog: id, tenantId → Tenant, productId → Product, notifiedToUserId → User, sentAt, status
137	
138	### Key Business Rules (CRITICAL — agents must enforce)
139	- Product.currentQuantity is system-managed only — never directly editable
140	- Product.sellingPrice is always computed: supplierCost + (supplierCost × markupPercent / 100)
141	- Stock can never go negative — any violating transaction must be blocked
142	- All stock quantity changes must be atomic — full transaction rollback on any failure
143	- Every quantity change must create a StockMovementLog with before/after values
144	- Every important action must create an AuditLog entry — immutable
145	- All tenant-scoped data must include tenantId — enforced by RLS at database level
146	- Users can never see, query, or modify another tenant's data
147	- When session.isImpersonating = true: all tRPC mutations return FORBIDDEN server-side regardless of role. Client UI reflects this with disabled controls and the impersonation banner.
148	- Unique constraints (productCode, barcodeValue, poNumber, referenceNumber, serialValue) are scoped per tenant, not globally
149	- Serial tracking applies only when product has serialTrackingEnabled = true
150	- Barcode/QR scanning is input helper only — all scanned values must pass server-side validation
151	- StockOut.printableSlipNumber format: 'SO-' + zero-padded 5-digit sequential number per tenant (e.g. SO-00001, SO-00002). Auto-generated at creation time, immutable, unique per tenant. The counter is never reset — numbers only ever increase.
152	
153	## Integrations
154	- Email (SMTP): daily low stock notification emails per tenant, welcome emails for new tenant admins — SES in production, MailHog in dev
155	- No external auth service — Auth.js v5 sessions stored in PostgreSQL (white-label, zero external dependency)
156	
157	## Deployment Config
158	Environments: dev / staging / prod
159	Hosting:      Single VPS or managed services
160	Dev mode:     MODE A — WSL2 native (pre-locked)
161	Docker Hub:   disabled
162	
163	## Mobile Needs
164	None — web only. The web app is mobile-first responsive (optimised for smartphones and tablets in warehouse environments) but there is no native mobile app.
165	
166	## Non-functional Requirements
167	Performance:    < 500ms API response for all CRUD operations. Stock transactions < 1s including all log writes. Tenant context resolution (slug → tenantId) < 10ms (cached).
168	Uptime:         99.5% SLA for production (SaaS — customer-facing)
169	Data retention: All traceability records kept minimum 7 years. Users disabled, never deleted. Tenants suspended, never deleted.
170	Compliance:     None specific. Standard data protection practices. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at database level via RLS.
171	
172	## Tenancy Model
173	multi — subdirectory routing
174	URL pattern: app.com/[tenant-slug]/dashboard, app.com/[tenant-slug]/products, etc.
175	Shared global data: none — all business data is tenant-scoped
176	Roles: global (same role set across all tenants: admin, warehouse_staff, purchasing_staff)
177	DB isolation: shared schema + tenant_id column on all tenant-scoped tables + PostgreSQL RLS
178	DB isolation exception: none (inventory data does not require separate schema)
179	
180	## User-Facing URLs
181	/login                              public — Auth.js v5 authentication
182	/platform/tenants                   super_admin — tenant management
183	/platform/metrics                   super_admin — cross-tenant metrics
184	/platform/audit-logs                super_admin — platform audit logs
185	/[tenant-slug]/dashboard            tenant — main dashboard
186	/[tenant-slug]/products             tenant — product list
187	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]        tenant — product detail
188	/[tenant-slug]/products/[id]/history tenant — product movement history
189	/[tenant-slug]/suppliers            tenant — supplier list
190	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders      tenant — PO list
191	/[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders/[id] tenant — PO detail
192	/[tenant-slug]/stock-in             tenant — stock receiving
193	/[tenant-slug]/stock-out            tenant — stock releasing
194	/[tenant-slug]/adjustments          tenant — stock adjustments (admin only)
195	/[tenant-slug]/reports              tenant — reports and exports
196	/[tenant-slug]/audit-logs           tenant — audit log viewer (admin only)
197	/[tenant-slug]/users                tenant — user management (admin only)
198	
199	## Access Control
```

 `./docs/PRODUCT.md`

```text
199	
200	## Access Control
201	Public routes:    /login (Auth.js v5 sign-in page)
202	Protected routes: all other routes require authenticated session via Auth.js v5
203	Super-admin only: /platform/* routes
204	Tenant admin only: /[tenant-slug]/users, /[tenant-slug]/audit-logs, /[tenant-slug]/adjustments
205	Tenant admin + purchasing: /[tenant-slug]/purchase-orders, /[tenant-slug]/suppliers
206	All tenant roles: /[tenant-slug]/dashboard, /[tenant-slug]/products, /[tenant-slug]/stock-in, /[tenant-slug]/stock-out, /[tenant-slug]/reports
207	
208	### Tenant isolation rules
209	- Authenticated user's tenantId is extracted from Auth.js v5 session/JWT on every request (L1)
210	- Every tenant-scoped DB query is automatically filtered by tenantId via Prisma middleware (L2)
211	- PostgreSQL RLS provides database-level enforcement as defense-in-depth
212	- A user assigned to tenant A can never access routes or data for tenant B
213	- super_admin has no tenantId — accesses only /platform/* routes, never tenant business data directly
214	
215	## Data Sensitivity
216	PII stored:       yes — user name, email, hashed password (in PostgreSQL via Auth.js v5), requestedByName
217	Financial data:   yes — supplierCost, markupPercent, sellingPrice, purchase order values, inventory valuation (restricted by role, scoped by tenant)
218	Health data:      no
219	Audit required:   tenant creation/suspension/reactivation, user creation/role changes/disabling, login events, product create/update/pricing/threshold changes, supplier create/update, PO create/update/status change, stock in, stock out, stock adjustment, serial number creation/status changes, file upload/removal, report export, notification send/failure, every quantity-affecting stock movement, super_admin impersonation events
220	GDPR/compliance:  no specific regulatory requirement — standard data protection. Cross-tenant data isolation enforced at DB level. 7-year retention. No hard deletes.
221	
222	## Security Requirements
223	Rate limiting:    public: 60/min per IP | authenticated: 300/min per user | upload: 10/min per user
224	CORS origins:     dev: localhost:* | staging: https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app | prod: https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
225	Security layers:  L1 app-layer tenant scoping (tenantId from Auth.js session/JWT) — ACTIVE
226	                  L2 Prisma auto-inject middleware (tenantId on every query) — ACTIVE
227	                  L3 RBAC tRPC middleware (role-based access on every endpoint) — ACTIVE
228	                  L4 PgBouncer per-tenant connection pool limits — ACTIVE
229	                  L5 AuditLog (immutable record on every mutation) — ACTIVE
230	                  L6 Prisma query guardrails (automatic data scoping) — ACTIVE
231	
232	### Inventory-specific security
233	- No negative stock — enforced at database transaction level
234	- Audit logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
235	- Stock movement logs immutable — no update or delete operations permitted
236	- Tenant admin cannot view other tenants' audit logs
237	- Warehouse staff cannot view supplier cost or markup pricing data
238	- All barcode/QR scanned values must pass server-side validation
239	- super_admin actions are audit-logged separately at platform level
240	
241	### Browser camera scanning
242	- Camera permission requested before use
243	- Client-side JavaScript barcode/QR library (html5-qrcode or zxing-js)
244	- Supported: Android Chrome, iOS Safari, laptop webcam
245	- Manual text input fallback always available
246	- Live scanner preview with clear success/error feedback
247	- Large tap targets for warehouse mobile use
248	
249	## Domain / Base URL Expectations
250	Dev:     http://localhost:[port assigned by Phase 3 — do not specify a number here]
251	Stage:   https://inventorize-staging.powerbyte.app
252	Prod:    https://inventorize.powerbyte.app
253	
254	## Infrastructure Notes
255	Default: all services run in Docker Compose — mono-server for dev/staging/prod.
256	Docker Hub publishing: disabled
257	pgAdmin: included on all environments — credentials auto-generated by Phase 3
258	CREDENTIALS.md: generated by Phase 3 — master credentials list for all envs, strictly gitignored
259	Security: HTTP headers + rate limiter + DOMPurify sanitizer scaffolded by Phase 4 — always-on defaults
260	Spec stress-test: Phase 2.7 runs automatically before Phase 3 — catches PRODUCT.md gaps early
261	AWS path when ready: RDS, S3, ElastiCache, SES — update .env.{env} only, zero code changes.
262	
263	### Compose services (dev)
264	- Next.js app (web server + tRPC API)
265	- PostgreSQL (primary database with RLS policies — also stores Auth.js v5 sessions)
266	- Valkey (cache + BullMQ job queue)
267	- MinIO (S3-compatible file storage for uploads)
268	- pgAdmin (database management UI)
269	- PgBouncer (connection pooling with per-tenant limits)
270	
271	### Production path
272	- Managed PostgreSQL with RLS (e.g. RDS, Supabase, or DigitalOcean Managed DB)
273	- S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, or DigitalOcean Spaces)
274	- Managed Redis/Valkey (e.g. ElastiCache, Upstash)
275	- PgBouncer (connection pooling)
276	- SES or equivalent for email notifications
277	
278	## Tech Stack Preferences
279	Frontend framework:        Next.js
280	API style:                 tRPC
281	ORM / DB layer:            Prisma
282	Auth provider:             Auth.js v5 (sessions in PostgreSQL — white-label, zero external service)
283	Auth strategy:             authjs
284	Account setup token:       VerificationToken table (Auth.js v5 schema compatible). Fields: identifier (user email), token (hashed), expires (DateTime). Used for first-login setup links (24h expiry, single-use).
285	Primary database:          PostgreSQL
286	Cache / queue:             Valkey + BullMQ
287	File storage:              MinIO (dev) / S3 (prod)
288	UI component library:      shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS
289	
290	## Design Identity
291	Brand feel:         professional/enterprise
292	Target aesthetic:   Clean, dense information layout optimised for fast warehouse operations. Minimal decorative elements. High contrast for readability in bright warehouse environments. Large tap targets and clear feedback for scanning workflows. Tenant branding kept minimal — platform-consistent UI.
293	Industry category:  SaaS / inventory management
294	Dark mode required: optional toggle
295	Key constraint:     Mobile-first responsive layout — warehouse staff primarily use smartphones and tablets. Scanner UI must have large tap targets and clear success/error feedback.
296	
297	## Out of Scope
298	- Multi-warehouse support per tenant (single location per tenant, no warehouse-to-warehouse transfers)
```
</details></tool-use>

// Enums — @inventorize/shared/enums
// All enum values derive from docs/PRODUCT.md Data Entities section

export const UserRole = {
  SUPER_ADMIN: 'super_admin',
  ADMIN: 'admin',
  WAREHOUSE_STAFF: 'warehouse_staff',
  PURCHASING_STAFF: 'purchasing_staff',
} as const;
export type UserRole = (typeof UserRole)[keyof typeof UserRole];

export const TenantStatus = {
  ACTIVE: 'active',
  SUSPENDED: 'suspended',
  TRIAL: 'trial',
} as const;
export type TenantStatus = (typeof TenantStatus)[keyof typeof TenantStatus];

export const POStatus = {
  DRAFT: 'draft',
  ORDERED: 'ordered',
  PARTIALLY_RECEIVED: 'partially_received',
  RECEIVED: 'received',
  CANCELLED: 'cancelled',
} as const;
export type POStatus = (typeof POStatus)[keyof typeof POStatus];

export const SerialNumberStatus = {
  IN_STOCK: 'in_stock',
  ISSUED: 'issued',
  ADJUSTED: 'adjusted',
} as const;
export type SerialNumberStatus = (typeof SerialNumberStatus)[keyof typeof SerialNumberStatus];

export const MovementType = {
  STOCK_IN: 'stock_in',
  STOCK_OUT: 'stock_out',
  ADJUSTMENT: 'adjustment',
} as const;
export type MovementType = (typeof MovementType)[keyof typeof MovementType];

export const NotificationStatus = {
  SENT: 'sent',
  FAILED: 'failed',
  RETRIED: 'retried',
} as const;
export type NotificationStatus = (typeof NotificationStatus)[keyof typeof NotificationStatus];

export const AdjustmentReason = {
  RECOUNT: 'recount',
  DAMAGE: 'damage',
  THEFT: 'theft',
  CORRECTION: 'correction',
  OTHER: 'other',
} as const;
export type AdjustmentReason = (typeof AdjustmentReason)[keyof typeof AdjustmentReason];

// Types — @inventorize/shared/types
// All interfaces derive from docs/PRODUCT.md Data Entities section

import type {
  UserRole,
  TenantStatus,
  POStatus,
  SerialNumberStatus,
  MovementType,
  NotificationStatus,
  AdjustmentReason,
} from '../enums/index.js';

// ─── Platform-level entities (no tenantId) ───────────────────

export interface Tenant {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  slug: string;
  contactEmail: string;
  status: TenantStatus;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface User {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string | null;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  hashedPassword: string;
  role: UserRole;
  isActive: boolean;
  lastLoginAt: Date | null;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface VerificationToken {
  identifier: string;
  token: string;
  expires: Date;
}

// ─── Tenant-scoped entities ──────────────────────────────────

export interface Product {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  productCode: string;
  barcodeValue: string;
  name: string;
  category: string;
  unit: string;
  supplierCost: number;
  markupPercent: number;
  sellingPrice: number;
  currentQuantity: number;
  lowStockThreshold: number;
  serialTrackingEnabled: boolean;
  isActive: boolean;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface Supplier {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  name: string;
  contactPerson: string | null;
  phone: string | null;
  email: string | null;
  address: string | null;
  notes: string | null;
  isActive: boolean;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface PurchaseOrder {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  poNumber: string;
  supplierId: string;
  orderDate: Date;
  expectedDate: Date | null;
  status: POStatus;
  notes: string | null;
  attachmentUrl: string | null;
  createdByUserId: string;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface PurchaseOrderItem {
  id: string;
  purchaseOrderId: string;
  productId: string;
  orderedQty: number;
  receivedQty: number;
  supplierCostSnapshot: number;
}

export interface StockIn {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  referenceNumber: string;
  purchaseOrderId: string | null;
  receivedDate: Date;
  receivedByUserId: string;
  notes: string | null;
  attachmentUrl: string | null;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export interface StockInItem {
  id: string;
  stockInId: string;
  productId: string;
  quantity: number;
  supplierCostSnapshot: number;
}

export interface StockOut {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  referenceNumber: string;
  releasedDate: Date;
  releasedByUserId: string;
  requestedByName: string;
  usedFor: string;
  notes: string | null;
  printableSlipNumber: string;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export interface StockOutItem {
  id: string;
  stockOutId: string;
  productId: string;
  quantity: number;
  sellingPriceSnapshot: number;
}

export interface SerialNumber {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  productId: string;
  serialValue: string;
  barcodeValue: string | null;
  status: SerialNumberStatus;
  stockInItemId: string | null;
  stockOutItemId: string | null;
  createdAt: Date;
  updatedAt: Date;
}

export interface StockAdjustment {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  adjustmentDate: Date;
  reason: AdjustmentReason;
  notes: string | null;
  createdByUserId: string;
  approvedByUserId: string | null;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export interface StockAdjustmentItem {
  id: string;
  stockAdjustmentId: string;
  productId: string;
  quantityDelta: number;
  serialNumberId: string | null;
}

export interface StockMovementLog {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  productId: string;
  movementType: MovementType;
  referenceType: string;
  referenceId: string;
  quantityBefore: number;
  quantityDelta: number;
  quantityAfter: number;
  serialNumberId: string | null;
  performedByUserId: string;
  requestedByName: string | null;
  usedFor: string | null;
  performedAt: Date;
  notes: string | null;
}

export interface AuditLog {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string | null;
  actorUserId: string;
  actionType: string;
  entityType: string;
  entityId: string;
  fieldChangesJson: unknown;
  beforeStateJson: unknown;
  afterStateJson: unknown;
  createdAt: Date;
}

export interface LowStockNotificationLog {
  id: string;
  tenantId: string;
  productId: string;
  notifiedToUserId: string;
  sentAt: Date;
  status: NotificationStatus;
}

// ─── Session type ────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface AppSession {
  userId: string;
  tenantId: string | null;
  roles: UserRole[];
  name: string;
  email: string;
  isImpersonating: boolean;
  impersonatedTenantId?: string | undefined;
  impersonatedTenantName?: string | undefined;
}

// ─── Pagination ──────────────────────────────────────────────

export interface PaginationInput {
  page: number;
  limit: number;
}

export interface PaginatedResult<T> {
  items: T[];
  total: number;
  page: number;
  limit: number;
  totalPages: number;
}

// Zod Schemas — @inventorize/shared/schemas
// Validation schemas for all entities — used by tRPC input validation
// All schemas derive from docs/PRODUCT.md Data Entities section

import { z } from 'zod';

// ─── Enum schemas ────────────────────────────────────────────

export const userRoleSchema = z.enum([
  'super_admin',
  'admin',
  'warehouse_staff',
  'purchasing_staff',
]);

export const tenantStatusSchema = z.enum(['active', 'suspended', 'trial']);

export const poStatusSchema = z.enum([
  'draft',
  'ordered',
  'partially_received',
  'received',
  'cancelled',
]);

export const serialNumberStatusSchema = z.enum(['in_stock', 'issued', 'adjusted']);

export const movementTypeSchema = z.enum(['stock_in', 'stock_out', 'adjustment']);

export const notificationStatusSchema = z.enum(['sent', 'failed', 'retried']);

export const adjustmentReasonSchema = z.enum([
  'recount',
  'damage',
  'theft',
  'correction',
  'other',
]);

// ─── Pagination ──────────────────────────────────────────────

export const paginationInputSchema = z.object({
  page: z.number().int().min(1).default(1),
  limit: z.number().int().min(1).max(200).default(50),
});

// ─── Tenant schemas ──────────────────────────────────────────

export const createTenantSchema = z
  .object({
    name: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim(),
    slug: z
      .string()
      .min(1)
      .max(100)
      .regex(/^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/, 'Slug must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens'),
    contactEmail: z.string().email().max(255),
  })
  .strict();

export const updateTenantStatusSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
    status: tenantStatusSchema,
  })
  .strict();

// ─── User schemas ────────────────────────────────────────────

export const createUserSchema = z
  .object({
    tenantId: z.string().cuid(),
    name: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim(),
    email: z.string().email().max(255),
    role: userRoleSchema.exclude(['super_admin']),
  })
  .strict();

export const updateUserSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
    name: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim().optional(),
    role: userRoleSchema.exclude(['super_admin']).optional(),
    isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .strict();

export const loginSchema = z
  .object({
    email: z.string().email().max(255),
    password: z.string().min(1).max(128),
  })
  .strict();

export const setupPasswordSchema = z
  .object({
    token: z.string().min(1),
    password: z.string().min(8).max(128),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Product schemas ─────────────────────────────────────────

export const createProductSchema = z
  .object({
    productCode: z.string().min(1).max(50).trim(),
    barcodeValue: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim(),
    name: z.string().min(1).max(200).trim(),
    category: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim(),
    unit: z.string().min(1).max(50).trim(),
    supplierCost: z.number().nonnegative(),
    markupPercent: z.number().nonnegative().max(10000),
    lowStockThreshold: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
    serialTrackingEnabled: z.boolean().default(false),
  })
  .strict();

export const updateProductSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
    productCode: z.string().min(1).max(50).trim().optional(),
    barcodeValue: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim().optional(),
    name: z.string().min(1).max(200).trim().optional(),
    category: z.string().min(1).max(100).trim().optional(),
    unit: z.string().min(1).max(50).trim().optional(),
    supplierCost: z.number().nonnegative().optional(),
    markupPercent: z.number().nonnegative().max(10000).optional(),
    lowStockThreshold: z.number().int().nonnegative().optional(),
    serialTrackingEnabled: z.boolean().optional(),
    isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Supplier schemas ────────────────────────────────────────

export const createSupplierSchema = z
  .object({
    name: z.string().min(1).max(200).trim(),
    contactPerson: z.string().max(100).trim().optional(),
    phone: z.string().max(50).trim().optional(),
    email: z.string().email().max(255).optional(),
    address: z.string().max(500).trim().optional(),
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
  })
  .strict();

export const updateSupplierSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
    name: z.string().min(1).max(200).trim().optional(),
    contactPerson: z.string().max(100).trim().optional(),
    phone: z.string().max(50).trim().optional(),
    email: z.string().email().max(255).optional(),
    address: z.string().max(500).trim().optional(),
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
    isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Purchase Order schemas ──────────────────────────────────

export const createPurchaseOrderSchema = z
  .object({
    supplierId: z.string().cuid(),
    orderDate: z.coerce.date(),
    expectedDate: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
    items: z
      .array(
        z
          .object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            orderedQty: z.number().int().positive(),
            supplierCostSnapshot: z.number().nonnegative(),
          })
          .strict(),
      )
      .min(1),
  })
  .strict();

export const updatePOStatusSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
    status: poStatusSchema,
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Stock In schemas ────────────────────────────────────────

export const createStockInSchema = z
  .object({
    purchaseOrderId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    receivedDate: z.coerce.date(),
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
    items: z
      .array(
        z
          .object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            quantity: z.number().int().positive(),
            supplierCostSnapshot: z.number().nonnegative(),
            serialNumbers: z.array(z.string().min(1).max(100).trim()).optional(),
          })
          .strict(),
      )
      .min(1),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Stock Out schemas ───────────────────────────────────────

export const createStockOutSchema = z
  .object({
    releasedDate: z.coerce.date(),
    requestedByName: z.string().min(1).max(200).trim(),
    usedFor: z.string().min(1).max(500).trim(),
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
    items: z
      .array(
        z
          .object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            quantity: z.number().int().positive(),
            sellingPriceSnapshot: z.number().nonnegative(),
            serialNumberIds: z.array(z.string().cuid()).optional(),
          })
          .strict(),
      )
      .min(1),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Stock Adjustment schemas ────────────────────────────────

export const createStockAdjustmentSchema = z
  .object({
    adjustmentDate: z.coerce.date(),
    reason: adjustmentReasonSchema,
    notes: z.string().max(1000).trim().optional(),
    approvedByUserId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    items: z
      .array(
        z
          .object({
            productId: z.string().cuid(),
            quantityDelta: z.number().int(),
            serialNumberId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
          })
          .strict(),
      )
      .min(1),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Filter / query schemas ─────────────────────────────────

export const productFilterSchema = z
  .object({
    search: z.string().max(200).optional(),
    category: z.string().max(100).optional(),
    isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
    lowStockOnly: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .strict()
  .merge(paginationInputSchema);

export const stockMovementFilterSchema = z
  .object({
    productId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    movementType: movementTypeSchema.optional(),
    performedByUserId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    dateFrom: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    dateTo: z.coerce.date().optional(),
  })
  .strict()
  .merge(paginationInputSchema);

export const auditLogFilterSchema = z
  .object({
    actionType: z.string().max(100).optional(),
    entityType: z.string().max(100).optional(),
    actorUserId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    dateFrom: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    dateTo: z.coerce.date().optional(),
  })
  .strict()
  .merge(paginationInputSchema);

export const purchaseOrderFilterSchema = z
  .object({
    status: poStatusSchema.optional(),
    supplierId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
    dateFrom: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    dateTo: z.coerce.date().optional(),
  })
  .strict()
  .merge(paginationInputSchema);

export const supplierFilterSchema = z
  .object({
    search: z.string().max(200).optional(),
    isActive: z.boolean().optional(),
  })
  .strict()
  .merge(paginationInputSchema);

// ─── Report export schema ────────────────────────────────────

export const reportExportSchema = z
  .object({
    reportType: z.enum([
      'low_stock',
      'stock_movement',
      'current_inventory',
      'product_history',
      'audit_trail',
    ]),
    dateFrom: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    dateTo: z.coerce.date().optional(),
    productId: z.string().cuid().optional(),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── Slug validation ─────────────────────────────────────────

export const checkSlugSchema = z
  .object({
    slug: z
      .string()
      .min(1)
      .max(100)
      .regex(/^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/),
  })
  .strict();

// ─── ID param schema (reusable) ─────────────────────────────

export const idParamSchema = z
  .object({
    id: z.string().cuid(),
  })
  .strict();

```

```typescript
// API Client — @inventorize/api-client
// Typed tRPC client for use by all apps (web, mobile if added later)
// This package provides the tRPC client setup and type-safe caller.
// The actual AppRouter type will be provided by the web app's tRPC router.

import { createTRPCClient, httpBatchLink } from '@trpc/client';
import type { AnyRouter } from '@trpc/server';
import superjson from 'superjson';

export type { AnyRouter } from '@trpc/server';

export interface CreateClientOptions {
  url: string;
  headers?: (() => Record<string, string>) | Record<string, string>;
}

/**
 * Creates a typed tRPC client instance.
 * Usage in apps:
 *   import type { AppRouter } from '@/server/trpc/router';
 *   import { createApiClient } from '@inventorize/api-client';
 *   const client = createApiClient<AppRouter>({ url: '/api/trpc' });
 */
export function createApiClient<TRouter extends AnyRouter>(options: CreateClientOptions) {
  return createTRPCClient<TRouter>({
    links: [
      httpBatchLink({
        url: options.url,
        headers: options.headers,
        transformer: superjson,
      }),
    ],
  });
}

// Re-export shared types and schemas for convenience
export type {
  Tenant,
  User,
  Product,
  Supplier,
  PurchaseOrder,
  PurchaseOrderItem,
  StockIn,
  StockInItem,
  StockOut,
  StockOutItem,
  SerialNumber,
  StockAdjustment,
  StockAdjustmentItem,
  StockMovementLog,
  AuditLog,
  LowStockNotificationLog,
  AppSession,
  PaginationInput,
  PaginatedResult,
} from '@inventorize/shared/types';

export {
  UserRole,
  TenantStatus,
  POStatus,
  SerialNumberStatus,
  MovementType,
  NotificationStatus,
  AdjustmentReason,
} from '@inventorize/shared/enums';

```

```text
I now have all the information needed. Let me compile the detailed plan.

---

## Phase 4 Part 3 Implementation Plan -- `packages/db`

### Overview

This plan covers generating the full `packages/db` workspace package containing: Prisma schema with all 15+ models, initial migration, seed script with the webmaster super_admin account, L5 audit log helper, L6 tenant-guard Prisma extension, and L2 RLS helper. The tenancy mode is **multi** (confirmed in `inputs.yml` line 19), meaning `tenantId` is **NOT NULL** on all tenant-scoped entities, RLS policies are **active** (not commented), and all 6 security layers are fully wired.

---

### File 1: `packages/db/package.json`

**Contents:**
- `name`: `@inventorize/db`
- `version`: `0.0.1`
- `private`: true
- `type`: `module` (matches `@inventorize/shared` convention)
- `exports` field exposing:
  - `"."` -> `./src/index.ts` (PrismaClient + types)
  - `"./audit"` -> `./src/audit.ts`
  - `"./middleware/tenant-guard"` -> `./src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`
  - `"./rls"` -> `./src/rls.ts`
- `scripts`:
  - `db:generate`: `prisma generate`
  - `db:migrate`: `prisma migrate dev`
  - `db:seed`: `tsx prisma/seed.ts`
  - `db:studio`: `prisma studio --port 44894` (port from `inputs.yml`)
  - `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit`
  - `lint`: `eslint src/`
- `dependencies`:
  - `@prisma/client`: `^6.5.0` (latest stable as of early 2026, compatible with Node 22)
  - `bcrypt` (or `bcryptjs` for pure-JS -- prefer `bcryptjs` to avoid native build issues on WSL2)
- `devDependencies`:
  - `prisma`: `^6.5.0` (must match `@prisma/client` version)
  - `tsx`: `^4.x` (for running seed.ts directly)
  - `typescript`: `^5.7.0` (match root)
- `prisma.seed` field: `tsx prisma/seed.ts` (Prisma recognizes this for `prisma db seed`)

**Gotchas:**
- Prisma v6 requires `@prisma/client` and `prisma` CLI to be the same major version. Do not mix v5 and v6.
- The `prisma` field in package.json should point to the schema: `"prisma": { "schema": "prisma/schema.prisma", "seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts" }`.
- Use `tsx` not `ts-node` since the project uses ESM (`"type": "module"`). `ts-node` has ESM pain; `tsx` just works.

---

### File 2: `packages/db/tsconfig.json`

**Contents:**
- Extends `../../tsconfig.base.json` (same pattern as `packages/shared/tsconfig.json`)
- `compilerOptions.outDir`: `./dist`
- `compilerOptions.rootDir`: `./src`
- `include`: `["src/**/*.ts", "prisma/seed.ts"]`
- `exclude`: `["node_modules", "dist"]`

**Gotcha:** The seed script lives in `prisma/seed.ts` not `src/`, so it must be explicitly included or it will fail typecheck. Alternatively, seed.ts can be run via `tsx` without being in the compiled output -- in that case, a separate `tsconfig.seed.json` or just relying on `tsx` (which does its own transform) is acceptable. The pragmatic approach: leave seed.ts out of `include` and let `tsx` handle it at runtime. Only `src/**/*.ts` in `include`.

---

### File 3: `packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma`

**Datasource and generator:**
- `datasource db`: provider `postgresql`, url `env("DATABASE_URL")`
- `generator client`: provider `prisma-client-js`, output default (node_modules/.prisma/client)

**Enum definitions in Prisma** (must mirror `@inventorize/shared/enums` exactly):
- `UserRole`: `super_admin`, `admin`, `warehouse_staff`, `purchasing_staff`
- `TenantStatus`: `active`, `suspended`, `trial`
- `POStatus`: `draft`, `ordered`, `partially_received`, `received`, `cancelled`
- `SerialNumberStatus`: `in_stock`, `issued`, `adjusted`
- `MovementType`: `stock_in`, `stock_out`, `adjustment`
- `NotificationStatus`: `sent`, `failed`, `retried`
- `AdjustmentReason`: `recount`, `damage`, `theft`, `correction`, `other`

**Models (15 total):**

1. **Tenant** (platform-level, no tenantId on self):
   - `id String @id @default(cuid())`
   - `name String`, `slug String @unique`, `contactEmail String @map("contact_email")`, `status TenantStatus @default(active)`, `createdAt DateTime @default(now())`, `updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt`
   - Relations: `users User[]`, `products Product[]`, etc. (all reverse relations)
   - `@@map("tenants")`

2. **User** (platform-level, tenantId nullable for super_admin):
   - `id String @id @default(cuid())`
   - `tenantId String? @map("tenant_id")`, `tenant Tenant? @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])`
   - `name`, `email String @unique`, `hashedPassword String @map("hashed_password")`, `role UserRole`, `isActive Boolean @default(true)`, `lastLoginAt DateTime? @map("last_login_at")`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([email])`
   - `@@map("users")`

3. **VerificationToken** (platform-level):
   - `identifier String` (email), `token String @unique` (hashed), `expires DateTime`
   - `@@unique([identifier, token])` (Auth.js v5 compatible compound key)
   - `@@map("verification_tokens")`

4. **Product** (tenant-scoped):
   - `id`, `tenantId String @map("tenant_id")`, `tenant Tenant @relation(...)` (NOT NULL -- multi mode)
   - `productCode`, `barcodeValue`, `name`, `category`, `unit`, `supplierCost Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`, `markupPercent Decimal @db.Decimal(8,2)`, `sellingPrice Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`, `currentQuantity Int @default(0)`, `lowStockThreshold Int`, `serialTrackingEnabled Boolean @default(false)`, `isActive Boolean @default(true)`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, productCode])`, `@@unique([tenantId, barcodeValue])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([tenantId, category])`
   - `@@map("products")`

5. **Supplier** (tenant-scoped):
   - Standard fields, nullable `contactPerson`, `phone`, `email`, `address`, `notes`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`
   - `@@map("suppliers")`

6. **PurchaseOrder** (tenant-scoped):
   - `poNumber`, `supplierId` -> Supplier, `orderDate`, `expectedDate?`, `status POStatus @default(draft)`, `notes?`, `attachmentUrl?`, `createdByUserId` -> User
   - `items PurchaseOrderItem[]`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, poNumber])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([supplierId])`, `@@index([tenantId, status])`
   - `@@map("purchase_orders")`

7. **PurchaseOrderItem**:
   - `id`, `purchaseOrderId` -> PurchaseOrder, `productId` -> Product, `orderedQty Int`, `receivedQty Int @default(0)`, `supplierCostSnapshot Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`
   - `@@index([purchaseOrderId])`, `@@index([productId])`
   - `@@map("purchase_order_items")`

8. **StockIn** (tenant-scoped):
   - `referenceNumber`, `purchaseOrderId?` -> PurchaseOrder, `receivedDate`, `receivedByUserId` -> User, `notes?`, `attachmentUrl?`, `createdAt`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`
   - `@@map("stock_ins")`

9. **StockInItem**:
   - `id`, `stockInId` -> StockIn, `productId` -> Product, `quantity Int`, `supplierCostSnapshot Decimal`
   - `@@index([stockInId])`, `@@index([productId])`
   - `@@map("stock_in_items")`

10. **StockOut** (tenant-scoped):
    - `referenceNumber`, `releasedDate`, `releasedByUserId` -> User, `requestedByName`, `usedFor`, `notes?`, `printableSlipNumber` (SO-00001 format)
    - `@@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])`, `@@unique([tenantId, printableSlipNumber])`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`
    - `@@map("stock_outs")`

11. **StockOutItem**:
    - `id`, `stockOutId` -> StockOut, `productId` -> Product, `quantity Int`, `sellingPriceSnapshot Decimal`
    - `@@map("stock_out_items")`

12. **SerialNumber** (tenant-scoped):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `serialValue`, `barcodeValue?`, `status SerialNumberStatus @default(in_stock)`, `stockInItemId?` -> StockInItem, `stockOutItemId?` -> StockOutItem, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
    - `@@unique([tenantId, productId, serialValue])` -- the critical composite unique
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`, `@@index([status])`
    - `@@map("serial_numbers")`

13. **StockAdjustment** (tenant-scoped):
    - `adjustmentDate`, `reason AdjustmentReason`, `notes?`, `createdByUserId` -> User, `approvedByUserId?` -> User, `createdAt`
    - `items StockAdjustmentItem[]`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`
    - `@@map("stock_adjustments")`

14. **StockAdjustmentItem**:
    - `id`, `stockAdjustmentId` -> StockAdjustment, `productId` -> Product, `quantityDelta Int`, `serialNumberId?` -> SerialNumber
    - `@@map("stock_adjustment_items")`

15. **StockMovementLog** (tenant-scoped, IMMUTABLE):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `movementType MovementType`, `referenceType String`, `referenceId String`, `quantityBefore Int`, `quantityDelta Int`, `quantityAfter Int`, `serialNumberId?` -> SerialNumber, `performedByUserId` -> User, `requestedByName?`, `usedFor?`, `performedAt DateTime`, `notes?`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`, `@@index([tenantId, referenceType, referenceId])`
    - `@@map("stock_movement_logs")`

16. **AuditLog** (IMMUTABLE, tenantId nullable for platform ops):
    - `id`, `tenantId?`, `actorUserId String @map("actor_user_id")`, `actionType String @map("action_type")`, `entityType String @map("entity_type")`, `entityId String @map("entity_id")`, `fieldChangesJson Json? @map("field_changes_json")`, `beforeStateJson Json? @map("before_state_json")`, `afterStateJson Json? @map("after_state_json")`, `createdAt`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([actorUserId])`, `@@index([entityType, entityId])`, `@@index([createdAt])`
    - `@@map("audit_logs")`

17. **LowStockNotificationLog** (tenant-scoped):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `notifiedToUserId` -> User, `sentAt DateTime`, `status NotificationStatus`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`
    - `@@map("low_stock_notification_logs")`

**Key design decisions for the schema:**

- Use `Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)` for all monetary fields (`supplierCost`, `sellingPrice`, `supplierCostSnapshot`, `sellingPriceSnapshot`, `markupPercent`). The shared types use `number`, but the DB must use precise decimals. This creates a known Prisma `Decimal` vs TypeScript `number` mismatch that consumers must handle.
- Use `@map("snake_case")` on all fields and `@@map("snake_case_table")` on all models. Prisma model names stay PascalCase, but the underlying PostgreSQL tables and columns use snake_case.
- `createdAt` / `updatedAt` use `@default(now())` / `@updatedAt` respectively.
- StockMovementLog and AuditLog have no `updatedAt` -- they are immutable.
- StockOut has no `updatedAt` -- it is immutable per spec.
- StockIn has no `updatedAt` -- only `createdAt` per the shared type definition.
- PurchaseOrderItem has no `createdAt`/`updatedAt` (matches shared types).

**Gotchas:**
- The `User` model has two potential relation conflicts: `createdByUserId` on PurchaseOrder, `receivedByUserId` on StockIn, `releasedByUserId` on StockOut, `createdByUserId` on StockAdjustment, `approvedByUserId` on StockAdjustment, `performedByUserId` on StockMovementLog, `notifiedToUserId` on LowStockNotificationLog, `actorUserId` on AuditLog. Each needs a unique relation name to disambiguate (e.g., `@relation("po_created_by")`, `@relation("stock_in_received_by")`, etc.).
- StockAdjustment has TWO User foreign keys (`createdByUserId` and `approvedByUserId`) -- both need distinct relation names.
- `VerificationToken` does not have an `id` field in the Auth.js v5 pattern; it uses `@@unique([identifier, token])` as compound key. However, the shared type does not include `id`. Check whether Prisma requires an `@id` -- if no single `@id`, use `@@id([identifier, token])` or add an explicit `id` field. Auth.js adapter expects `@@unique([identifier, token])` with no `id`. Prisma requires either `@id` or `@@id`. The standard Auth.js Prisma adapter uses `@@id([identifier, token])` -- follow that pattern.

---

### File 4: `packages/db/src/index.ts`

**Contents:**
- Import `PrismaClient` from `@prisma/client`
- Create and export a singleton `prisma` instance with global caching pattern (standard Next.js + Prisma pattern to avoid connection pool exhaustion in dev hot-reload)
- Re-export all Prisma generated types: `export type { Prisma, ... } from '@prisma/client'`
- Export the `tenantGuardExtension` applied client as a separate export for tenant-scoped use
- Export a `platformPrisma` instance WITHOUT the tenant-guard extension for superadmin operations (per CLAUDE.md line 1187-1191)

**Pattern:**
```
globalThis.__prisma (dev singleton) -> base PrismaClient
export const prisma = base.$extends(tenantGuardExtension(...))  // for tenant routers
export const platformPrisma = base  // for superadmin routers -- NO tenant guard
```

**Gotcha:** The tenant-guard extension needs a way to receive the current `tenantId` at runtime. This cannot be set at module scope. Options: (a) AsyncLocalStorage to carry tenantId per request, (b) pass tenantId via a function parameter each time. The CLAUDE.md example uses `currentTenantId()` which implies AsyncLocalStorage. This file should export a `setCurrentTenantId` / `getCurrentTenantId` pair backed by `AsyncLocalStorage`.

---

### File 5: `packages/db/src/audit.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `writeAuditLog` function matching the signature from CLAUDE.md line 3322-3324
- Parameters: `tx` (Prisma TransactionClient), plus an object with `tenantId` (string | null for platform ops), `userId`, `action` (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), `entity` (table/model name), `entityId`, `before` (Json | null), `after` (Json | null)
- Optionally compute `fieldChangesJson` by diffing `before` and `after` objects (shallow diff of changed keys)
- The function does a simple `tx.auditLog.create(...)` -- no conditional logic
- Mark the function as fire-and-forget safe (it should never throw and break the parent transaction -- wrap in try/catch with console.error fallback)

**Gotcha:** The `fieldChangesJson` field in the shared types exists but the CLAUDE.md AuditLog model example only shows `before` and `after` Json fields. The schema should include all three (`fieldChangesJson`, `beforeStateJson`, `afterStateJson`) to match the shared types. The audit helper should compute `fieldChangesJson` automatically from the diff.

---

### File 6: `packages/db/src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `tenantGuardExtension` using `Prisma.defineExtension(...)` with the `$allOperations` pattern from CLAUDE.md lines 3334-3351
- The skip list for non-tenant models: `['AuditLog', 'Tenant', 'VerificationToken']` -- VerificationToken is also platform-level (no tenantId)
- The `User` model is special: `tenantId` is nullable (super_admin has null). The guard should still inject tenantId for User queries when a tenantId is present, but must allow null for platform queries. This means User should probably be in the skip list, with explicit tenantId handling in the superadmin vs tenant routers.
- Uses `getCurrentTenantId()` from an AsyncLocalStorage-backed context (imported from `../index.ts` or a separate `context.ts`)
- For `where` injection: merge `tenantId` into existing where clause
- For `data` injection on create/createMany: merge `tenantId` into data
- For `createMany` with array data: iterate and inject into each item

**Gotchas:**
- CLAUDE.md line 1078 warns: L6 injects tenantId at TOP-LEVEL only. Nested includes follow FKs directly. This is inherent to the `$allOperations` approach and is documented, not a bug.
- `$allOperations` in Prisma extensions covers all operations including `count`, `aggregate`, `groupBy` -- this is the correct approach per CLAUDE.md line 3331-3333 which explicitly says DO NOT replace `$allOperations` with individual methods.
- Must handle `createMany` data being an array vs single object.
- If `getCurrentTenantId()` returns null/undefined, the guard should throw an error (not silently proceed without tenant scoping) -- this prevents accidental unscoped queries.

---

### File 7: `packages/db/src/rls.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `withTenant<T>` function matching CLAUDE.md lines 3377-3385
- Takes `tenantId: string` and a callback `fn: (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => Promise<T>`
- Opens a Prisma `$transaction`, runs `SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id` via `$executeRaw`, then calls `fn(tx)`
- `SET LOCAL` is transaction-scoped (auto-resets when transaction ends) -- safe for connection pooling

**Additionally:** The initial migration must include RLS policy SQL. Since tenancy mode is **multi**, these are ACTIVE (not commented). For each tenant-scoped table:
```sql
ALTER TABLE "table_name" ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON "table_name"
  USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::text);
```

Tables needing RLS (all tenant-scoped): `products`, `suppliers`, `purchase_orders`, `purchase_order_items` (via join to purchase_orders), `stock_ins`, `stock_in_items` (via join), `stock_outs`, `stock_out_items` (via join), `serial_numbers`, `stock_adjustments`, `stock_adjustment_items` (via join), `stock_movement_logs`, `low_stock_notification_logs`.

**Gotcha on child tables:** `purchase_order_items`, `stock_in_items`, `stock_out_items`, `stock_adjustment_items` do NOT have a direct `tenantId` column. RLS policies on these require either: (a) adding `tenantId` to these tables (denormalized but simpler for RLS), or (b) using a subquery-based policy that joins to the parent. Option (a) is strongly preferred for performance and simplicity -- add `tenantId` to all child/item tables even though the shared types do not include it. This is a DB-level concern; the shared types represent API-facing shapes. Alternatively, rely solely on L6 (Prisma guard on parent) and skip RLS on child tables, but this violates defence-in-depth.

**Recommendation:** Add `tenantId` to `PurchaseOrderItem`, `StockInItem`, `StockOutItem`, and `StockAdjustmentItem` in the Prisma schema (even though the shared TS types do not include it). The L6 tenant guard will auto-inject it on create. RLS can then use a simple `USING (tenant_id = ...)` on every table uniformly.

---

### File 8: `packages/db/prisma/seed.ts`

**Contents:**
1. Import `PrismaClient` (direct, not the tenant-guarded one)
2. Import `bcryptjs` (or `bcrypt`) for password hashing
3. Create seed data:

   **a. Default Tenant** (for dev):
   - name: `"Powerbyte Demo"`, slug: `"demo"`, contactEmail: `"demo@inventorize.local"`, status: `active`

   **b. Webmaster super_admin** (MANDATORY -- from CREDENTIALS.md):
   - email: `"webmaster@inventorize.local"`
   - name: `"Webmaster"`
   - role: `super_admin`
   - tenantId: `null` (super_admin is platform-level)
   - hashedPassword: bcrypt hash of `"dUx8ayzFLC5FHyauKaDE8e"` (from CREDENTIALS.md line 12)
   - isActive: `true`
   - The plaintext password must NOT appear in the seed script -- instead, the seed script should read it from `process.env` or hash a value passed via env var. However, per CLAUDE.md, the bcrypt hash can be hardcoded in the seed script (line 1511: "bcrypt hash written to seed script"). So: pre-compute the bcrypt hash and embed it as a constant string.

   **c. Demo admin user** (for dev testing):
   - email: `"admin@demo.inventorize.local"`
   - role: `admin`
   - tenantId: linked to the demo tenant
   - Password: AI-generated, documented in CREDENTIALS.md

   **d. Demo warehouse_staff and purchasing_staff users** (for dev testing)

   **e. Sample products, suppliers** (for dev convenience)

4. Use `prisma.$transaction` for atomicity
5. Use `upsert` with unique fields to make seed idempotent (safe to re-run)

**Gotchas:**
- CLAUDE.md line 1085-1088: Every seeded record MUST have a valid tenantId matching its tenant. Every FK must resolve within the same tenant. Critical for multi-tenant correctness.
- The seed password from CREDENTIALS.md is `dUx8ayzFLC5FHyauKaDE8e` (22 chars). This must be bcrypt-hashed at build time. Use cost factor 12 (standard).
- Seed script runs via `tsx` (ESM). Import syntax must use ESM (`import` not `require`).
- The `printableSlipNumber` counter for StockOut should start fresh (no seed data for stock_outs, or if seeded, start from SO-00001).

---

### Migration Strategy

The initial migration should be generated via `prisma migrate dev --name init` after the schema is written. This produces the SQL in `prisma/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_init/migration.sql`.

After the auto-generated migration, a second migration (`prisma migrate dev --name rls_policies`) should add the RLS policies as raw SQL. This keeps the RLS concern separate from the schema migration.

The RLS migration must also create the `app.current_tenant_id` GUC (Grand Unified Configuration) setting or ensure `current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')` works. PostgreSQL `current_setting` with a missing variable throws an error by default. Use `current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', true)` (the second arg `true` means "return NULL if missing") in the policy, OR ensure the application always sets it before any query (which `withTenant` handles).

Also: the Prisma migration user (the DB_USER from .env.dev) must be able to `ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`. This requires table ownership or superuser. In dev, the user is likely the table owner (since Prisma created the tables). In production, verify this.

**Important RLS gotcha:** Prisma's connection pool uses a single database role. RLS policies using `current_setting` with `SET LOCAL` work correctly only within `withTenant` transactions. Queries outside a `withTenant` block (e.g., direct `prisma.product.findMany()`) will NOT have the GUC set and will fail the RLS policy (returning zero rows or erroring). This is actually desirable as defence-in-depth: it forces all tenant-scoped queries through `withTenant`. But it means the seed script and superadmin queries need to either: (a) bypass RLS (use a superuser role or `SET ROLE`), or (b) use `withTenant`. The practical solution: make the Prisma migration user a PostgreSQL superuser in dev (typical), and in production, use `ALTER ROLE prisma_user SET row_security = off` or grant BYPASSRLS to the migration user. For the application connection, do NOT bypass RLS.

---

### Dependency and Sequencing Summary

1. Write `packages/db/package.json` and `packages/db/tsconfig.json` first
2. Run `pnpm install` from root to link the new workspace
3. Write `prisma/schema.prisma` with all models
4. Run `pnpm --filter @inventorize/db db:generate` to generate the Prisma client
5. Write `src/index.ts`, `src/audit.ts`, `src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`, `src/rls.ts`
6. Run `pnpm typecheck` to verify everything compiles
7. Start PostgreSQL via Docker Compose (must be running)
8. Run `prisma migrate dev --name init` to create initial migration
9. Manually add RLS policy migration or create a second migration
10. Write `prisma/seed.ts`
11. Run `pnpm --filter @inventorize/db db:seed`
12. Verify with `pnpm typecheck` from root

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Prisma Decimal vs TypeScript number**: The shared types use `number` for monetary fields but Prisma generates `Decimal` (from `@prisma/client/runtime`). Consumers will need `.toNumber()` calls or a mapping layer. This is a known friction point but the correct approach for financial data integrity.

2. **AsyncLocalStorage for tenant context**: Node 22 has stable `AsyncLocalStorage`. The implementation must ensure the ALS store is set in the request middleware (tRPC context) before any DB call. The `packages/db` package defines the ALS; the tRPC layer sets it.

3. **Child table tenantId denormalization**: Adding `tenantId` to item/child tables (`PurchaseOrderItem`, `StockInItem`, `StockOutItem`, `StockAdjustmentItem`) diverges from the shared types. This is acceptable -- the Prisma schema is the DB truth; shared types are the API contract. The API layer can omit `tenantId` from responses for child entities.

4. **VerificationToken without id**: Auth.js v5 Prisma adapter expects `@@id([identifier, token])`. Verify this works with the version of `@auth/prisma-adapter` that will be used in Part 5.

5. **exactOptionalPropertyTypes**: With this tsconfig flag, optional properties cannot be set to `undefined` explicitly -- they must be omitted. This affects how Prisma's generated types interact with the codebase. Prisma client types may set optional fields to `undefined` which can cause type errors. Watch for this during typecheck.

---

```text
1	# SPEC-DRIVEN PLATFORM — V26
2	
3	> **WHAT THIS FILE IS**
4	> This is the master prompt for building TypeScript apps with AI agents.
5	> It works for any app — web, mobile, admin panel, API — any size, any team.
6	>
7	> **HOW TO USE IT**
8	> - For **Claude Code**: save this file as `CLAUDE.md` at your project root.
9	>   Claude Code reads it automatically every session. No pasting needed.
10	> - For **Cline**: save this file as `CLAUDE.md`. Cline reads it via `.clinerules`.
11	>   Cline runs Phase 4 Part-by-Part (one Part per fresh session), then Phase 5–8
12	>   on explicit human trigger. No auto-chaining between phases.
13	> - For **Copilot chat**: paste this entire file as your first message each session.
14	> - For **Claude.ai chat**: paste this entire file as your first message.
15	>
16	> **THE ONE RULE YOU MUST REMEMBER**
17	> `docs/PRODUCT.md` is the ONLY file you ever edit as a human.
18	> Everything else — source code, database migrations, config files, CI — is
19	> owned by the agents. You never touch those files directly.
20	> You describe what you want in PRODUCT.md. The agents build it.
21	>
22	> **THE SIX AGENTS AND WHAT EACH ONE DOES**
23	> ```
24	> Claude Code        → Planning only. You use this to write and update PRODUCT.md.
25	>                      Auto-loads CLAUDE.md every session. Best for Phase 2 interview.
26	>
27	> Cline              → Building everything. Phase 4 Part-by-Part, Phase 5–8 on human trigger.
28	>                      Reads .clinerules. Phase 4 = Part-by-Part (Rule 24) — one Part per session.
29	>                      Human says "Start Phase 5", "Start Phase 6" after each completes.
30	>                      Default model: MiniMax M2.5 via OpenRouter (free tier). See Tool Setup Guide for full model routing.
31	>
32	> Copilot            → Inline autocomplete while you type (always on).
33	>                      Fallback if Cline hits an error it cannot resolve.
34	>                      PR reviews on GitHub. Changes attributed via SpecStory capture.
35	>
36	> SpecStory          → Passive change capture layer (V11+).
37	>                      Auto-saves every Claude Code + Cline session to .specstory/history/.
38	>                      Captures Copilot inline edits and manual changes too.
39	>                      Powers Governance Sync reconciliation and cross-agent attribution.
40	>                      Install once: SpecStory VS Code extension. Zero config needed.
41	>
42	> SocratiCode        → Semantic codebase intelligence MCP server (V10).
43	>                      Hybrid semantic + keyword search across the entire codebase.
44	>                      Polyglot dependency graph. Searches non-code artifacts too.
45	>                      61% less context, 84% fewer tool calls, 37x faster than grep.
46	>                      Runs as a persistent local service via npx — zero project overhead.
47	>
48	> code-review-graph  → Structural blast-radius MCP server (NEW V13).
49	>                      Builds a persistent Tree-sitter graph of your codebase.
50	>                      Answers "what breaks if I change this?" — not just "where is it?".
51	>                      Used alongside SocratiCode: semantic search + structural impact.
52	>                      Install once per machine: claude plugin add tirth8205/code-review-graph
53	>                      Dev/Test machine only — not staging, not production.
54	> ```
55	
56	---
57	
58	## WHO YOU ARE (AGENT ROLE)
59	
60	You are a **Spec-Driven Platform Architect** operating under **V26 STRICTEST** discipline.
61	
62	---
63	
64	### GLOBAL INSTRUCTION PRIORITY ORDER — MANDATORY
65	
66	When any two sources conflict, follow the higher-priority source. No exceptions.
67	
68	```
69	PRIORITY  SOURCE                        ROLE
70	────────  ────────────────────────────  ──────────────────────────────────────
71	1         Safety constraints            Never expose credentials, never delete
72	                                        without confirm, never harm user data
73	2         CLAUDE.md rules               This file — all 30 rules
74	3         Active phase rules            The numbered steps of the current phase
75	4         docs/PRODUCT.md               Feature intent — what to build
76	5         docs/DECISIONS_LOG.md         Locked decisions — never re-decide
77	6         inputs.yml                    Tech stack config
78	7         .github/skills/[pack]/SKILL.md Domain knowledge only — NEVER overrides
79	                                        governance or CLAUDE.md rules
80	8         User message instructions     Current session request
81	```
82	
83	**On conflict:** follow the higher-priority source + append to agent-log.md:
84	`CONFLICT: [source A] vs [source B] — followed [source A] per priority order`
85	
86	---
87	
88	Your non-negotiable behaviors:
89	- You follow every rule in this prompt without exception.
90	- You never skip governance steps even if the user asks.
91	- You never generate files without reading all required context documents first.
92	- You never modify `.devcontainer` after the initial scaffold (no devcontainer in this system — MODE A WSL2 native is the only supported dev environment).
93	- You never infer or assume missing information — you always ask. EXCEPTION: if the answer is already in DECISIONS_LOG.md, use it. Never re-ask a locked decision.
94	- You never hardcode tech stack choices — everything derives from `inputs.yml`.
95	- The entire codebase is **TypeScript end-to-end** — no JavaScript in src or apps.
96	- `docs/PRODUCT.md` is the ONLY file a human ever edits. Agents own everything else.
97	- Every `docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md` entry must include which agent made the change.
98	- **Search before reading (Rule 17)**: use `codebase_search` before opening files.
99	- **Typed lessons (Rule 18)**: read 🔴 gotchas and 🟤 decisions in lessons.md first.
100	- **SpecStory is the passive memory layer (Rule 19)**: Governance Sync reads it for unattributed changes.
```
</details></tool-use>

330:This system uses **MODE A (WSL2 native) exclusively**. There is no MODE B. There is no devcontainer.
332:**Why:** Devcontainer on WSL2 + Docker Desktop adds 4 layers of virtualisation (Windows → WSL2 → Docker Desktop → devcontainer). Every layer is a source of permission errors, EACCES failures, shell server crashes, and socket mount failures. MODE A eliminates all of this.
337:- Do NOT reference DinD (Docker-in-Docker) — not needed in WSL2 native
338:- All `docker compose` commands run from the WSL2 Ubuntu terminal directly
339:- Docker Desktop provides the Docker socket to WSL2 natively — no socket mounts needed

1073:3. ALL writes enforce ownership + tenant constraints — L6 auto-injects, but verify manually on sensitive ops.
1078:   L6 injects tenantId into the TOP-LEVEL query only. Nested includes (e.g. { include: { customer: true } })
1082:     (i.e. the related record was created within the same tenant — enforced by L6 on create).

1188:   WITHOUT the L6 tenant-guard extension — never an inline if/else in resolvers.

1545	  ## BOOTSTRAP — 🔴 WSL2 + Docker Desktop known pitfalls
1546	  - Type:      🔴 gotcha

1549	  - Concepts:  wsl2, docker-desktop, pnpm, nvm, permissions
1550	  - Narrative: Real failures on WSL2 + Docker Desktop. All fixes baked into Bootstrap template.
1551	    (1) Never use corepack enable — use npm install -g pnpm. corepack symlinks fail in some WSL2 setups.
1552	    (2) pnpm install must run from WSL2 terminal — not Windows PowerShell or CMD.
1553	    (3) Docker Desktop must be running before any docker compose command. Check with: docker ps.
1554	    (4) Port conflicts: dev services use non-standard random ports (Rule 22). If conflict occurs,
1555	        regenerate ports in inputs.yml → run Phase 7 → restart services.
1556	    (5) nvm must be sourced in .bashrc — add: [ -s "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh" ] && \. "$NVM_DIR/nvm.sh"
1557	    (6) WSL2 file permissions: always develop inside WSL2 filesystem (/home/user/) not /mnt/c/.
1558	        Working in /mnt/c/ causes severe pnpm and docker performance issues.
1559	  # ---
1560	
1561	Step 6 — .cline/memory/agent-log.md
1562	  Cline writes agent-log with correct format header.
1563	
1564	Step 7 — .claude/settings.json

1566	  (lessons.md listed first, matching Rule 4 read order).
1567	
1568	Step 8 — Bootstrap files
1569	  .gitignore initial content (CREDENTIALS.md MUST be here from day 1 — before any other step commits):
```

3319	- `src/audit.ts` — AuditLog write helper (L5 — always active):
3320	  ```ts
3321	  // Immutable audit record on every mutation — active in single AND multi mode
3322	  export async function writeAuditLog(tx, {
3323	    tenantId, userId, action, entity, entityId, before, after
3324	  }: AuditLogEntry): Promise<void>
3325	  ```
3326	
3327	- `src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts` — Prisma query guardrails (L6 — always active):
3328	  ```ts
3329	  // L6 — Prisma extension that auto-injects tenantId into EVERY query operation.
3330	  // CRITICAL: uses $allOperations to cover findMany, findFirst, findUnique, create,
3331	  // createMany, update, updateMany, delete, deleteMany, count, aggregate, groupBy.
3332	  // DO NOT replace $allOperations with a list of individual methods — any unlisted
3333	  // method becomes an unguarded tenant bypass.
3334	  export const tenantGuardExtension = Prisma.defineExtension({
3335	    query: {
3336	      $allModels: {
3337	        async $allOperations({ args, query, model, operation }) {
3338	          // Skip system tables that are not tenant-scoped
3339	          if (['AuditLog', 'Tenant'].includes(model)) return query(args);
3340	          // Inject tenantId into where clause for reads and writes
3341	          if ('where' in args) {
3342	            args.where = { ...args.where, tenantId: currentTenantId() };
3343	          }
3344	          if ('data' in args && !Array.isArray(args.data)) {
3345	            args.data = { ...args.data, tenantId: currentTenantId() };
3346	          }
3347	          return query(args);
3348	        },
3349	      }
3350	    }
3351	  });
3352	  ```
3353	
3354	- `AuditLog` Prisma model — always in schema:
3355	  ```prisma
3356	  model AuditLog {
3357	    id        String   @id @default(cuid())
3358	    tenantId  String?  @map("tenant_id")
3359	    userId    String   @map("user_id")
3360	    action    String   // CREATE | UPDATE | DELETE
3361	    entity    String   // table name
3362	    entityId  String   @map("entity_id")
3363	    before    Json?
3364	    after     Json?
3365	    createdAt DateTime @default(now())
3366	
3367	    @@index([tenantId])
3368	    @@index([userId])
3369	    @@index([entity, entityId])
3370	  }
3371	  ```
3372

3377	  export async function withTenant<T>(
3378	    tenantId: string,
3379	    fn: (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => Promise<T>
3380	  ): Promise<T> {
3381	    return prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {
3382	      await tx.$executeRaw`SELECT set_config('app.current_tenant_id', ${tenantId}, true)`;
3383	      return fn(tx);
3384	    });
3385	  }
3386	  ```
3387	- RLS migration (active, not commented):
3388	  ```sql
3389	  ALTER TABLE "Entity" ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
3390	  CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON "Entity"
3391	    USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::uuid);
3392	  ```
3393	
3394	**If `tenancy.mode: single` — write RLS as SQL comments for future upgrade:**

3398	  -- CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON "Entity"
3399	  --   USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::uuid);
