<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./CLAUDE.md`

```text
240	Tenancy is controlled by `tenancy.mode: single | multi` in `inputs.yml`.
241	
242	#### 7A — Always shared schema + tenant_id
243	
244	One database, one schema, tenant isolation via `tenant_id` column.
245	Never separate databases or schemas per tenant.
246	Exception: modules handling payroll, banking, or medical data MAY use a separate PostgreSQL schema per tenant — but ONLY when explicitly declared in PRODUCT.md AND confirmed during Phase 2.7 stress-test. This exception must be written to DECISIONS_LOG.md before Phase 3. If not declared in PRODUCT.md, Cline MUST use shared schema — no exceptions, no guessing.
247	
248	#### 7B — Single-tenant scaffold
249	
250	Even in single mode, ALL entities get `tenantId` as a nullable UUID field
251	and RLS policies written as SQL comments (not yet active).
252	
253	Security layers — always active vs deferred in single mode:
254	```
255	L1 — tRPC tenantId scoping    DEFERRED   (only meaningful with 2+ tenants)
256	L2 — PostgreSQL RLS           DEFERRED   (written as comments, enabled on upgrade)
257	L3 — RBAC middleware          ACTIVE     (prevents privilege escalation in any app)
258	L4 — PgBouncer pool limits    DEFERRED   (only meaningful with 2+ tenants)
259	L5 — Immutable AuditLog       ACTIVE     (every mutation logged — privacy + traceability)
260	L6 — Prisma query guardrails  ACTIVE     (prevents developer mistakes from leaking data)
261	```
262	
263	L3, L5, L6 are always active — single or multi. Upgrading to multi only activates
264	L1, L2, L4 which are already scaffolded but dormant. No new columns, no table rewrites.
265	
266	Prisma pattern (single mode):
267	```prisma
268	model Entity {
269	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
270	  // DO NOT REMOVE — enables zero-migration upgrade to multi-tenant
271	  tenantId  String?  @map("tenant_id")
272	  tenant    Tenant?  @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
273	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
274	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
275	  @@index([tenantId])
276	}
277	```
278	
279	#### 7C — Multi-tenant scaffold
280	
281	When `tenancy.mode: multi`:
282	- `tenantId` is NOT NULL on every entity
283	- RLS policies enabled (not commented)
284	- All 6 security layers fully wired (L1–L6)
285	- JWT always includes `{ userId, tenantId, roles[] }`
286	
287	Prisma pattern (multi mode):
288	```prisma
289	model Entity {
290	  id       String @id @default(cuid())
291	  tenantId String @map("tenant_id")   // NOT NULL in multi mode
292	  tenant   Tenant @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
293	  // ... entity fields
294	  @@index([tenantId])
295	}
296	```
297	
298	Tenant table always scaffolded (single and multi):
299	```prisma
300	model Tenant {
301	  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
302	  name      String
303	  slug      String   @unique  // used for subdirectory or subdomain routing
304	  isActive  Boolean  @default(true)
305	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
306	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
307	}
308	```
309	In single mode: one Tenant row seeded in the seed script.
310	In multi mode: Tenant rows created via admin onboarding flow.
311	
312	#### 7D — Upgrade path: single → multi
313	
314	Trigger: change `tenancy.mode` in PRODUCT.md → run Feature Update.
315	⚠️ Run data migration BEFORE schema migration — otherwise NOT NULL fails on existing rows.
316	
317	#### 7E — All 6 security layers (multi mode — all required)
318	
319	```
```
</details></tool-use>

2	# Format: ## YYYY-MM-DD — [Decision title]

7	## Dev Environment Mode
8	- Type:      🟤 decision
9	- Date:      2026-04-02
10	- Decision:  MODE A — WSL2 native (the only supported mode as of V25)
11	- Rationale: Devcontainer adds 4 virtualisation layers on WSL2 + Docker Desktop causing
12	             permission errors, shell server crashes, and socket failures. WSL2 native eliminates all of this.
13	             Docker Desktop provides the Docker socket to WSL2 natively. No DinD needed.
14	- Locked:    yes — do not re-ask or scaffold devcontainer files.
15

23	             Feature branches use feat/{slug}. Never commit directly to main.
24	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 23

33	             Execution: minimax-m2.5 (default — free tier, Cline via OpenRouter)
34	             Governance: gemini-2.5-flash-lite (cheapest, non-critical writes)
35	- Locked:    yes — follows Rule 24

43	             DB=44874, PgBouncer=44875, Redis=44876, MinIO=44877, MinIO Console=44878,
44	             MailHog SMTP=44879, MailHog UI=44880, pgAdmin=44881, App=44884, Worker=44885, Studio=44894
45	- Rationale: Rule 22 — unique random ports per project prevent conflicts on developer machines.
46	             Standard ports (5432, 6379, etc.) reserved for staging/prod.

1070	2. Handle race conditions with optimistic locking or SELECT FOR UPDATE:
1071	   → Inventory decrements: read quantity → check > 0 → decrement in transaction → retry on conflict.
1072	   → Billing/credits: same pattern. Never decrement without a transactional guard.
1073	3. ALL writes enforce ownership + tenant constraints — L6 auto-injects, but verify manually on sensitive ops.
1074	4. Define unique constraints in Prisma schema wherever business logic requires uniqueness.
1075	5. ALL foreign key relationships enforced — no orphaned records allowed.
1076	6. Critical operations (payments, role changes, deletions) MUST be idempotent — safe to retry.
1077	7. Prisma include/select with nested relations — SECURITY WARNING:
1078	   L6 injects tenantId into the TOP-LEVEL query only. Nested includes (e.g. { include: { customer: true } })
1079	   follow foreign keys directly — they do NOT re-check tenantId on the related record.
1080	   → For sensitive data: use separate queries with explicit tenantId on each related entity.
1081	   → For non-sensitive data: nested includes are acceptable IF the foreign key integrity is guaranteed
1082	     (i.e. the related record was created within the same tenant — enforced by L6 on create).
1083	   → NEVER use nested includes to fetch records from a configurable or user-editable foreign key
1084	     without verifying the related record's tenantId matches.
1085	8. Seed script tenant isolation — MANDATORY:
1086	   Every seeded record MUST have a valid tenantId that matches the tenant it belongs to.
1087	   Every foreign key in seed data MUST resolve to a record within the SAME tenant.
1088	   NEVER create a record in Tenant A that references a record in Tenant B.
1089	   Validate: after seeding, run a query that joins every FK and checks tenantId matches on both sides.
1090	9. Data migration scripts and one-off patches — tenant safety:
1091	   ANY script that runs outside of tRPC (prisma db execute, seed scripts, data patches):
1092	   → MUST iterate over tenants explicitly: for each tenant → scope all queries to that tenant.
1093	   → MUST NOT use unscoped updateMany/deleteMany across all tenants.
1094	   → MUST log which tenant each operation affected.
1095	   → Test on a single tenant first before running across all tenants.
1096	10. Export and report queries — tenant scoping MANDATORY:
1097	    CSV exports, PDF reports, dashboard aggregations, and analytics queries
1098	    MUST include tenantId in every WHERE clause — even on count() and aggregate().
1099	    L6 covers these if using $allOperations (V25), but add explicit tenantId as defence-in-depth.
1100	    An export without tenant scoping leaks every tenant's data into one file.
1101	```
1102	
1103	**FILE UPLOAD SAFETY — enforce when packages/storage/ is generated:**
1104	```
1105	1. Whitelist allowed MIME types explicitly — reject everything else. Default: images + PDF + common docs.
1106	2. Validate MIME type server-side (magic bytes) — NEVER trust file extension alone.
1107	3. Enforce file size limits per upload type. Default max: 10 MB. Configurable in inputs.yml.
1108	4. Randomize stored filenames — never use the original user-submitted filename.
1109	5. Storage paths MUST include tenantId: ${tenantId}/${entityType}/${randomFilename}.
1110	6. SVG and HTML uploads are BLOCKED by default — they can contain embedded JavaScript (XSS vector).
1111	7. Uploaded files MUST NOT be served with executable content-type headers.
1112	8. File DOWNLOAD endpoints MUST verify the requesting user's tenantId matches the file's storage path prefix.
1113	   → Extract tenantId from the storage key (first path segment).
1114	   → Compare against ctx.tenantId from the authenticated session.
1115	   → If mismatch → return 404 (not 403 — do not confirm the file exists).
1116	   → NEVER serve a file by accepting a raw storage key from the client without this check.
1117	```
1118	
1119	**QUEUE AND CACHE SAFETY — enforce when packages/jobs/ is generated:**
1120	```
1121	1. ALL BullMQ job payloads MUST include tenantId and userId — no tenant-blind jobs.
1122	2. Workers MUST validate tenantId before processing — reject jobs with missing or invalid tenantId.
1123	3. ALL Valkey cache keys MUST be prefixed with tenantId: ${tenantId}:${keyName}.
1124	4. Jobs MUST be idempotent — safe to retry on failure without side effects.
1125	5. No PII, passwords, or tokens stored in plaintext in job payloads or cache values.
1126	6. DLQ (dead letter queue) entries MUST be tenant-scoped and never expose cross-tenant data.
1127	7. Cron jobs and scheduled tasks — SPECIAL HANDLING:
1128	   Cron jobs run on a timer — they have NO HTTP request, NO session, NO user, NO tenant context.
1129	   → A cron job MUST iterate over all active tenants explicitly:
1130	     const tenants = await prisma.tenant.findMany({ where: { isActive: true } });
1131	     for (const tenant of tenants) {
1132	       await processForTenant(tenant.id);  // every query inside scoped to this tenantId
1133	     }
1134	   → NEVER run a cron job that queries without tenant context — it returns data from ALL tenants.
1135	   → Log each tenant iteration separately for audit trail.
1136	```
1137	
1138	**PRODUCTION ERROR HANDLING — enforce in every tRPC router:**
1139	```
1140	1. NEVER return raw Prisma errors, stack traces, or internal details to the client.
1141	2. Wrap all tRPC error responses in generic messages for production:
1142	   → BAD_REQUEST: "Invalid input."
1143	   → NOT_FOUND: "Resource not found."
1144	   → FORBIDDEN: "Access denied."
1145	   → INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR: "Something went wrong. Please try again."
1146	3. Log the FULL error (including stack trace) server-side for debugging.
1147	4. NEVER include table names, column names, or constraint names in client-facing errors.
1148	5. Auth error messages MUST NOT reveal whether the account, tenant, or email exists:
1149	   → Login failure: "Invalid credentials." (not "User not found" or "Wrong password" or "Organization not found")
1150	   → Password reset: "If that email exists, a reset link has been sent." (not "Email not found")
1151	   → Tenant resolution: "Access denied." (not "Organization does not exist")
1152	   This prevents enumeration attacks where an attacker discovers valid emails, usernames, or tenant slugs
1153	   by observing different error messages for existing vs non-existing values.
1154	```
1155	
1156	**WEBHOOK SAFETY — enforce when any external integration is implemented:**
1157	```
1158	1. ALL incoming webhooks MUST verify the provider's signature before processing.
1159	2. Webhook handlers MUST be idempotent — duplicate delivery must not cause duplicate side effects.
1160	3. Webhook secrets MUST be stored in environment variables only — never hardcoded.
1161	```
1162	
1163	**AUTH DEFAULTS — do not override these Auth.js v5 secure defaults:**
1164	```
1165	1. Session cookies: HttpOnly=true, Secure=true (production), SameSite=lax. Do NOT change these.
1166	2. Password reset tokens: MUST be time-limited (max 1 hour) and single-use.
1167	3. Email verification: enforce before any privileged action (role assignment, data export, billing).
1168	4. Logout: MUST invalidate the session server-side — not just clear the frontend cookie.
1169	5. Auth secrets: MUST exist in env vars only. NEVER import or reference in any client-side file.
1170	```
1171	
1172	**TENANT MIDDLEWARE SAFETY — enforce in src/middleware.ts:**
1173	```
1174	1. URL-derived tenant slug (from path or subdomain) MUST be cross-checked against the session.
1175	   → After resolving the tenant from the URL, verify session.user.tenantId === resolved tenant ID.
1176	   → If mismatch → redirect to the user's actual tenant URL. Do NOT serve the requested tenant's data.
1177	   → This prevents the tenant-switching attack: user types /other-tenant/settings in the URL bar
1178	     and gets access to another tenant because middleware trusts the URL without session verification.
1179	2. If the user has access to multiple tenants (e.g. superadmin):
1180	   → The session MUST contain the CURRENTLY ACTIVE tenantId, not a list of all accessible tenants.
1181	   → Tenant switching MUST go through an explicit API call that updates the session's active tenantId.
1182	   → The URL slug is a display convenience — the session is the source of truth for tenant context.
1183	```
1184	
1185	**SUPERADMIN AND PLATFORM-LEVEL ROLES — enforce when any cross-tenant role exists:**
1186	```
1187	1. Superadmin queries that bypass tenant scoping MUST use a dedicated Prisma client instance
1188	   WITHOUT the L6 tenant-guard extension — never an inline if/else in resolvers.
1189	   → Create: const platformPrisma = new PrismaClient();  // NO tenant extension
1190	   → Use platformPrisma ONLY in superadmin-specific routers.
1191	   → Regular tenant-scoped routers continue using the guarded prisma instance.
1192	2. ALL superadmin operations MUST be logged to AuditLog with action prefix "PLATFORM:" to distinguish
1193	   from tenant-scoped operations. Example: "PLATFORM:VIEW_ALL_TENANTS", "PLATFORM:DISABLE_TENANT".
1194	3. Superadmin routers MUST be in a separate tRPC router file (e.g. src/server/trpc/routers/platform.ts)
1195	   with its own middleware that checks for the platform-level role — never mixed into tenant routers.
1196	4. NEVER add an inline `if (isSuperadmin) skip tenant filter` inside a regular tenant-scoped resolver.
1197	   This pattern spreads across every resolver over time and becomes impossible to audit.
1198	```

I now have all the information needed. Let me compile the detailed plan.

---

## Phase 4 Part 3 Implementation Plan -- `packages/db`

### Overview

This plan covers generating the full `packages/db` workspace package containing: Prisma schema with all 15+ models, initial migration, seed script with the webmaster super_admin account, L5 audit log helper, L6 tenant-guard Prisma extension, and L2 RLS helper. The tenancy mode is **multi** (confirmed in `inputs.yml` line 19), meaning `tenantId` is **NOT NULL** on all tenant-scoped entities, RLS policies are **active** (not commented), and all 6 security layers are fully wired.

---

### File 1: `packages/db/package.json`

**Contents:**
- `name`: `@inventorize/db`
- `version`: `0.0.1`
- `private`: true
- `type`: `module` (matches `@inventorize/shared` convention)
- `exports` field exposing:
  - `"."` -> `./src/index.ts` (PrismaClient + types)
  - `"./audit"` -> `./src/audit.ts`
  - `"./middleware/tenant-guard"` -> `./src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`
  - `"./rls"` -> `./src/rls.ts`
- `scripts`:
  - `db:generate`: `prisma generate`
  - `db:migrate`: `prisma migrate dev`
  - `db:seed`: `tsx prisma/seed.ts`
  - `db:studio`: `prisma studio --port 44894` (port from `inputs.yml`)
  - `typecheck`: `tsc --noEmit`
  - `lint`: `eslint src/`
- `dependencies`:
  - `@prisma/client`: `^6.5.0` (latest stable as of early 2026, compatible with Node 22)
  - `bcrypt` (or `bcryptjs` for pure-JS -- prefer `bcryptjs` to avoid native build issues on WSL2)
- `devDependencies`:
  - `prisma`: `^6.5.0` (must match `@prisma/client` version)
  - `tsx`: `^4.x` (for running seed.ts directly)
  - `typescript`: `^5.7.0` (match root)
- `prisma.seed` field: `tsx prisma/seed.ts` (Prisma recognizes this for `prisma db seed`)

**Gotchas:**
- Prisma v6 requires `@prisma/client` and `prisma` CLI to be the same major version. Do not mix v5 and v6.
- The `prisma` field in package.json should point to the schema: `"prisma": { "schema": "prisma/schema.prisma", "seed": "tsx prisma/seed.ts" }`.
- Use `tsx` not `ts-node` since the project uses ESM (`"type": "module"`). `ts-node` has ESM pain; `tsx` just works.

---

### File 2: `packages/db/tsconfig.json`

**Contents:**
- Extends `../../tsconfig.base.json` (same pattern as `packages/shared/tsconfig.json`)
- `compilerOptions.outDir`: `./dist`
- `compilerOptions.rootDir`: `./src`
- `include`: `["src/**/*.ts", "prisma/seed.ts"]`
- `exclude`: `["node_modules", "dist"]`

**Gotcha:** The seed script lives in `prisma/seed.ts` not `src/`, so it must be explicitly included or it will fail typecheck. Alternatively, seed.ts can be run via `tsx` without being in the compiled output -- in that case, a separate `tsconfig.seed.json` or just relying on `tsx` (which does its own transform) is acceptable. The pragmatic approach: leave seed.ts out of `include` and let `tsx` handle it at runtime. Only `src/**/*.ts` in `include`.

---

### File 3: `packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma`

**Datasource and generator:**
- `datasource db`: provider `postgresql`, url `env("DATABASE_URL")`
- `generator client`: provider `prisma-client-js`, output default (node_modules/.prisma/client)

**Enum definitions in Prisma** (must mirror `@inventorize/shared/enums` exactly):
- `UserRole`: `super_admin`, `admin`, `warehouse_staff`, `purchasing_staff`
- `TenantStatus`: `active`, `suspended`, `trial`
- `POStatus`: `draft`, `ordered`, `partially_received`, `received`, `cancelled`
- `SerialNumberStatus`: `in_stock`, `issued`, `adjusted`
- `MovementType`: `stock_in`, `stock_out`, `adjustment`
- `NotificationStatus`: `sent`, `failed`, `retried`
- `AdjustmentReason`: `recount`, `damage`, `theft`, `correction`, `other`

**Models (15 total):**

1. **Tenant** (platform-level, no tenantId on self):
   - `id String @id @default(cuid())`
   - `name String`, `slug String @unique`, `contactEmail String @map("contact_email")`, `status TenantStatus @default(active)`, `createdAt DateTime @default(now())`, `updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt`
   - Relations: `users User[]`, `products Product[]`, etc. (all reverse relations)
   - `@@map("tenants")`

2. **User** (platform-level, tenantId nullable for super_admin):
   - `id String @id @default(cuid())`
   - `tenantId String? @map("tenant_id")`, `tenant Tenant? @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])`
   - `name`, `email String @unique`, `hashedPassword String @map("hashed_password")`, `role UserRole`, `isActive Boolean @default(true)`, `lastLoginAt DateTime? @map("last_login_at")`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([email])`
   - `@@map("users")`

3. **VerificationToken** (platform-level):
   - `identifier String` (email), `token String @unique` (hashed), `expires DateTime`
   - `@@unique([identifier, token])` (Auth.js v5 compatible compound key)
   - `@@map("verification_tokens")`

4. **Product** (tenant-scoped):
   - `id`, `tenantId String @map("tenant_id")`, `tenant Tenant @relation(...)` (NOT NULL -- multi mode)
   - `productCode`, `barcodeValue`, `name`, `category`, `unit`, `supplierCost Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`, `markupPercent Decimal @db.Decimal(8,2)`, `sellingPrice Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`, `currentQuantity Int @default(0)`, `lowStockThreshold Int`, `serialTrackingEnabled Boolean @default(false)`, `isActive Boolean @default(true)`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, productCode])`, `@@unique([tenantId, barcodeValue])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([tenantId, category])`
   - `@@map("products")`

5. **Supplier** (tenant-scoped):
   - Standard fields, nullable `contactPerson`, `phone`, `email`, `address`, `notes`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`
   - `@@map("suppliers")`

6. **PurchaseOrder** (tenant-scoped):
   - `poNumber`, `supplierId` -> Supplier, `orderDate`, `expectedDate?`, `status POStatus @default(draft)`, `notes?`, `attachmentUrl?`, `createdByUserId` -> User
   - `items PurchaseOrderItem[]`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, poNumber])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([supplierId])`, `@@index([tenantId, status])`
   - `@@map("purchase_orders")`

7. **PurchaseOrderItem**:
   - `id`, `purchaseOrderId` -> PurchaseOrder, `productId` -> Product, `orderedQty Int`, `receivedQty Int @default(0)`, `supplierCostSnapshot Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)`
   - `@@index([purchaseOrderId])`, `@@index([productId])`
   - `@@map("purchase_order_items")`

8. **StockIn** (tenant-scoped):
   - `referenceNumber`, `purchaseOrderId?` -> PurchaseOrder, `receivedDate`, `receivedByUserId` -> User, `notes?`, `attachmentUrl?`, `createdAt`
   - `@@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])`
   - `@@index([tenantId])`
   - `@@map("stock_ins")`

9. **StockInItem**:
   - `id`, `stockInId` -> StockIn, `productId` -> Product, `quantity Int`, `supplierCostSnapshot Decimal`
   - `@@index([stockInId])`, `@@index([productId])`
   - `@@map("stock_in_items")`

10. **StockOut** (tenant-scoped):
    - `referenceNumber`, `releasedDate`, `releasedByUserId` -> User, `requestedByName`, `usedFor`, `notes?`, `printableSlipNumber` (SO-00001 format)
    - `@@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])`, `@@unique([tenantId, printableSlipNumber])`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`
    - `@@map("stock_outs")`

11. **StockOutItem**:
    - `id`, `stockOutId` -> StockOut, `productId` -> Product, `quantity Int`, `sellingPriceSnapshot Decimal`
    - `@@map("stock_out_items")`

12. **SerialNumber** (tenant-scoped):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `serialValue`, `barcodeValue?`, `status SerialNumberStatus @default(in_stock)`, `stockInItemId?` -> StockInItem, `stockOutItemId?` -> StockOutItem, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
    - `@@unique([tenantId, productId, serialValue])` -- the critical composite unique
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`, `@@index([status])`
    - `@@map("serial_numbers")`

13. **StockAdjustment** (tenant-scoped):
    - `adjustmentDate`, `reason AdjustmentReason`, `notes?`, `createdByUserId` -> User, `approvedByUserId?` -> User, `createdAt`
    - `items StockAdjustmentItem[]`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`
    - `@@map("stock_adjustments")`

14. **StockAdjustmentItem**:
    - `id`, `stockAdjustmentId` -> StockAdjustment, `productId` -> Product, `quantityDelta Int`, `serialNumberId?` -> SerialNumber
    - `@@map("stock_adjustment_items")`

15. **StockMovementLog** (tenant-scoped, IMMUTABLE):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `movementType MovementType`, `referenceType String`, `referenceId String`, `quantityBefore Int`, `quantityDelta Int`, `quantityAfter Int`, `serialNumberId?` -> SerialNumber, `performedByUserId` -> User, `requestedByName?`, `usedFor?`, `performedAt DateTime`, `notes?`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`, `@@index([tenantId, referenceType, referenceId])`
    - `@@map("stock_movement_logs")`

16. **AuditLog** (IMMUTABLE, tenantId nullable for platform ops):
    - `id`, `tenantId?`, `actorUserId String @map("actor_user_id")`, `actionType String @map("action_type")`, `entityType String @map("entity_type")`, `entityId String @map("entity_id")`, `fieldChangesJson Json? @map("field_changes_json")`, `beforeStateJson Json? @map("before_state_json")`, `afterStateJson Json? @map("after_state_json")`, `createdAt`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([actorUserId])`, `@@index([entityType, entityId])`, `@@index([createdAt])`
    - `@@map("audit_logs")`

17. **LowStockNotificationLog** (tenant-scoped):
    - `id`, `tenantId`, `productId` -> Product, `notifiedToUserId` -> User, `sentAt DateTime`, `status NotificationStatus`
    - `@@index([tenantId])`, `@@index([productId])`
    - `@@map("low_stock_notification_logs")`

**Key design decisions for the schema:**

- Use `Decimal @db.Decimal(12,2)` for all monetary fields (`supplierCost`, `sellingPrice`, `supplierCostSnapshot`, `sellingPriceSnapshot`, `markupPercent`). The shared types use `number`, but the DB must use precise decimals. This creates a known Prisma `Decimal` vs TypeScript `number` mismatch that consumers must handle.
- Use `@map("snake_case")` on all fields and `@@map("snake_case_table")` on all models. Prisma model names stay PascalCase, but the underlying PostgreSQL tables and columns use snake_case.
- `createdAt` / `updatedAt` use `@default(now())` / `@updatedAt` respectively.
- StockMovementLog and AuditLog have no `updatedAt` -- they are immutable.
- StockOut has no `updatedAt` -- it is immutable per spec.
- StockIn has no `updatedAt` -- only `createdAt` per the shared type definition.
- PurchaseOrderItem has no `createdAt`/`updatedAt` (matches shared types).

**Gotchas:**
- The `User` model has two potential relation conflicts: `createdByUserId` on PurchaseOrder, `receivedByUserId` on StockIn, `releasedByUserId` on StockOut, `createdByUserId` on StockAdjustment, `approvedByUserId` on StockAdjustment, `performedByUserId` on StockMovementLog, `notifiedToUserId` on LowStockNotificationLog, `actorUserId` on AuditLog. Each needs a unique relation name to disambiguate (e.g., `@relation("po_created_by")`, `@relation("stock_in_received_by")`, etc.).
- StockAdjustment has TWO User foreign keys (`createdByUserId` and `approvedByUserId`) -- both need distinct relation names.
- `VerificationToken` does not have an `id` field in the Auth.js v5 pattern; it uses `@@unique([identifier, token])` as compound key. However, the shared type does not include `id`. Check whether Prisma requires an `@id` -- if no single `@id`, use `@@id([identifier, token])` or add an explicit `id` field. Auth.js adapter expects `@@unique([identifier, token])` with no `id`. Prisma requires either `@id` or `@@id`. The standard Auth.js Prisma adapter uses `@@id([identifier, token])` -- follow that pattern.

### File 4: `packages/db/src/index.ts`

**Contents:**
- Import `PrismaClient` from `@prisma/client`
- Create and export a singleton `prisma` instance with global caching pattern (standard Next.js + Prisma pattern to avoid connection pool exhaustion in dev hot-reload)
- Re-export all Prisma generated types: `export type { Prisma, ... } from '@prisma/client'`
- Export the `tenantGuardExtension` applied client as a separate export for tenant-scoped use
- Export a `platformPrisma` instance WITHOUT the tenant-guard extension for superadmin operations (per CLAUDE.md line 1187-1191)

**Pattern:**
```
globalThis.__prisma (dev singleton) -> base PrismaClient
export const prisma = base.$extends(tenantGuardExtension(...))  // for tenant routers
export const platformPrisma = base  // for superadmin routers -- NO tenant guard
```

**Gotcha:** The tenant-guard extension needs a way to receive the current `tenantId` at runtime. This cannot be set at module scope. Options: (a) AsyncLocalStorage to carry tenantId per request, (b) pass tenantId via a function parameter each time. The CLAUDE.md example uses `currentTenantId()` which implies AsyncLocalStorage. This file should export a `setCurrentTenantId` / `getCurrentTenantId` pair backed by `AsyncLocalStorage`.

---

### File 5: `packages/db/src/audit.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `writeAuditLog` function matching the signature from CLAUDE.md line 3322-3324
- Parameters: `tx` (Prisma TransactionClient), plus an object with `tenantId` (string | null for platform ops), `userId`, `action` (CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE), `entity` (table/model name), `entityId`, `before` (Json | null), `after` (Json | null)
- Optionally compute `fieldChangesJson` by diffing `before` and `after` objects (shallow diff of changed keys)
- The function does a simple `tx.auditLog.create(...)` -- no conditional logic
- Mark the function as fire-and-forget safe (it should never throw and break the parent transaction -- wrap in try/catch with console.error fallback)

**Gotcha:** The `fieldChangesJson` field in the shared types exists but the CLAUDE.md AuditLog model example only shows `before` and `after` Json fields. The schema should include all three (`fieldChangesJson`, `beforeStateJson`, `afterStateJson`) to match the shared types. The audit helper should compute `fieldChangesJson` automatically from the diff.

---

### File 6: `packages/db/src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `tenantGuardExtension` using `Prisma.defineExtension(...)` with the `$allOperations` pattern from CLAUDE.md lines 3334-3351
- The skip list for non-tenant models: `['AuditLog', 'Tenant', 'VerificationToken']` -- VerificationToken is also platform-level (no tenantId)
- The `User` model is special: `tenantId` is nullable (super_admin has null). The guard should still inject tenantId for User queries when a tenantId is present, but must allow null for platform queries. This means User should probably be in the skip list, with explicit tenantId handling in the superadmin vs tenant routers.
- Uses `getCurrentTenantId()` from an AsyncLocalStorage-backed context (imported from `../index.ts` or a separate `context.ts`)
- For `where` injection: merge `tenantId` into existing where clause
- For `data` injection on create/createMany: merge `tenantId` into data
- For `createMany` with array data: iterate and inject into each item

**Gotchas:**
- CLAUDE.md line 1078 warns: L6 injects tenantId at TOP-LEVEL only. Nested includes follow FKs directly. This is inherent to the `$allOperations` approach and is documented, not a bug.
- `$allOperations` in Prisma extensions covers all operations including `count`, `aggregate`, `groupBy` -- this is the correct approach per CLAUDE.md line 3331-3333 which explicitly says DO NOT replace `$allOperations` with individual methods.
- Must handle `createMany` data being an array vs single object.
- If `getCurrentTenantId()` returns null/undefined, the guard should throw an error (not silently proceed without tenant scoping) -- this prevents accidental unscoped queries.

---

### File 7: `packages/db/src/rls.ts`

**Contents:**
- Export `withTenant<T>` function matching CLAUDE.md lines 3377-3385
- Takes `tenantId: string` and a callback `fn: (tx: Prisma.TransactionClient) => Promise<T>`
- Opens a Prisma `$transaction`, runs `SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id` via `$executeRaw`, then calls `fn(tx)`
- `SET LOCAL` is transaction-scoped (auto-resets when transaction ends) -- safe for connection pooling

**Additionally:** The initial migration must include RLS policy SQL. Since tenancy mode is **multi**, these are ACTIVE (not commented). For each tenant-scoped table:
```sql
ALTER TABLE "table_name" ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
CREATE POLICY tenant_isolation ON "table_name"
  USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')::text);
```

Tables needing RLS (all tenant-scoped): `products`, `suppliers`, `purchase_orders`, `purchase_order_items` (via join to purchase_orders), `stock_ins`, `stock_in_items` (via join), `stock_outs`, `stock_out_items` (via join), `serial_numbers`, `stock_adjustments`, `stock_adjustment_items` (via join), `stock_movement_logs`, `low_stock_notification_logs`.

**Gotcha on child tables:** `purchase_order_items`, `stock_in_items`, `stock_out_items`, `stock_adjustment_items` do NOT have a direct `tenantId` column. RLS policies on these require either: (a) adding `tenantId` to these tables (denormalized but simpler for RLS), or (b) using a subquery-based policy that joins to the parent. Option (a) is strongly preferred for performance and simplicity -- add `tenantId` to all child/item tables even though the shared types do not include it. This is a DB-level concern; the shared types represent API-facing shapes. Alternatively, rely solely on L6 (Prisma guard on parent) and skip RLS on child tables, but this violates defence-in-depth.

**Recommendation:** Add `tenantId` to `PurchaseOrderItem`, `StockInItem`, `StockOutItem`, and `StockAdjustmentItem` in the Prisma schema (even though the shared TS types do not include it). The L6 tenant guard will auto-inject it on create. RLS can then use a simple `USING (tenant_id = ...)` on every table uniformly.

---

**Contents:**
1. Import `PrismaClient` (direct, not the tenant-guarded one)
2. Import `bcryptjs` (or `bcrypt`) for password hashing
3. Create seed data:

   **a. Default Tenant** (for dev):
   - name: `"Powerbyte Demo"`, slug: `"demo"`, contactEmail: `"demo@inventorize.local"`, status: `active`

   **b. Webmaster super_admin** (MANDATORY -- from CREDENTIALS.md):
   - email: `"webmaster@inventorize.local"`
   - name: `"Webmaster"`
   - role: `super_admin`
   - tenantId: `null` (super_admin is platform-level)
   - hashedPassword: bcrypt hash of `"dUx8ayzFLC5FHyauKaDE8e"` (from CREDENTIALS.md line 12)
   - isActive: `true`
   - The plaintext password must NOT appear in the seed script -- instead, the seed script should read it from `process.env` or hash a value passed via env var. However, per CLAUDE.md, the bcrypt hash can be hardcoded in the seed script (line 1511: "bcrypt hash written to seed script"). So: pre-compute the bcrypt hash and embed it as a constant string.

   **c. Demo admin user** (for dev testing):
   - email: `"admin@demo.inventorize.local"`
   - role: `admin`
   - tenantId: linked to the demo tenant
   - Password: AI-generated, documented in CREDENTIALS.md

   **d. Demo warehouse_staff and purchasing_staff users** (for dev testing)

   **e. Sample products, suppliers** (for dev convenience)

4. Use `prisma.$transaction` for atomicity
5. Use `upsert` with unique fields to make seed idempotent (safe to re-run)

**Gotchas:**
- CLAUDE.md line 1085-1088: Every seeded record MUST have a valid tenantId matching its tenant. Every FK must resolve within the same tenant. Critical for multi-tenant correctness.
- The seed password from CREDENTIALS.md is `dUx8ayzFLC5FHyauKaDE8e` (22 chars). This must be bcrypt-hashed at build time. Use cost factor 12 (standard).
- Seed script runs via `tsx` (ESM). Import syntax must use ESM (`import` not `require`).
- The `printableSlipNumber` counter for StockOut should start fresh (no seed data for stock_outs, or if seeded, start from SO-00001).

---

### Migration Strategy

The initial migration should be generated via `prisma migrate dev --name init` after the schema is written. This produces the SQL in `prisma/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_init/migration.sql`.

After the auto-generated migration, a second migration (`prisma migrate dev --name rls_policies`) should add the RLS policies as raw SQL. This keeps the RLS concern separate from the schema migration.

The RLS migration must also create the `app.current_tenant_id` GUC (Grand Unified Configuration) setting or ensure `current_setting('app.current_tenant_id')` works. PostgreSQL `current_setting` with a missing variable throws an error by default. Use `current_setting('app.current_tenant_id', true)` (the second arg `true` means "return NULL if missing") in the policy, OR ensure the application always sets it before any query (which `withTenant` handles).

Also: the Prisma migration user (the DB_USER from .env.dev) must be able to `ALTER TABLE ... ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY`. This requires table ownership or superuser. In dev, the user is likely the table owner (since Prisma created the tables). In production, verify this.

**Important RLS gotcha:** Prisma's connection pool uses a single database role. RLS policies using `current_setting` with `SET LOCAL` work correctly only within `withTenant` transactions. Queries outside a `withTenant` block (e.g., direct `prisma.product.findMany()`) will NOT have the GUC set and will fail the RLS policy (returning zero rows or erroring). This is actually desirable as defence-in-depth: it forces all tenant-scoped queries through `withTenant`. But it means the seed script and superadmin queries need to either: (a) bypass RLS (use a superuser role or `SET ROLE`), or (b) use `withTenant`. The practical solution: make the Prisma migration user a PostgreSQL superuser in dev (typical), and in production, use `ALTER ROLE prisma_user SET row_security = off` or grant BYPASSRLS to the migration user. For the application connection, do NOT bypass RLS.

---

### Dependency and Sequencing Summary

1. Write `packages/db/package.json` and `packages/db/tsconfig.json` first
2. Run `pnpm install` from root to link the new workspace
3. Write `prisma/schema.prisma` with all models
4. Run `pnpm --filter @inventorize/db db:generate` to generate the Prisma client
5. Write `src/index.ts`, `src/audit.ts`, `src/middleware/tenant-guard.ts`, `src/rls.ts`
6. Run `pnpm typecheck` to verify everything compiles
7. Start PostgreSQL via Docker Compose (must be running)
8. Run `prisma migrate dev --name init` to create initial migration
9. Manually add RLS policy migration or create a second migration
10. Write `prisma/seed.ts`
11. Run `pnpm --filter @inventorize/db db:seed`
12. Verify with `pnpm typecheck` from root

---

### Potential Challenges

1. **Prisma Decimal vs TypeScript number**: The shared types use `number` for monetary fields but Prisma generates `Decimal` (from `@prisma/client/runtime`). Consumers will need `.toNumber()` calls or a mapping layer. This is a known friction point but the correct approach for financial data integrity.

2. **AsyncLocalStorage for tenant context**: Node 22 has stable `AsyncLocalStorage`. The implementation must ensure the ALS store is set in the request middleware (tRPC context) before any DB call. The `packages/db` package defines the ALS; the tRPC layer sets it.

3. **Child table tenantId denormalization**: Adding `tenantId` to item/child tables (`PurchaseOrderItem`, `StockInItem`, `StockOutItem`, `StockAdjustmentItem`) diverges from the shared types. This is acceptable -- the Prisma schema is the DB truth; shared types are the API contract. The API layer can omit `tenantId` from responses for child entities.

4. **VerificationToken without id**: Auth.js v5 Prisma adapter expects `@@id([identifier, token])`. Verify this works with the version of `@auth/prisma-adapter` that will be used in Part 5.

5. **exactOptionalPropertyTypes**: With this tsconfig flag, optional properties cannot be set to `undefined` explicitly -- they must be omitted. This affects how Prisma's generated types interact with the codebase. Prisma client types may set optional fields to `undefined` which can cause type errors. Watch for this during typecheck.

```prisma
// Prisma Schema — Inventorize
// Multi-tenant SaaS with shared schema + tenant_id + PostgreSQL RLS
// All tenant-scoped models have tenantId (NOT NULL in multi mode)
// Child/item tables include tenantId for uniform RLS policies

datasource db {
  provider = "postgresql"
  url      = env("DATABASE_URL")
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client-js"
}

// ─── Enums ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

enum UserRole {
  super_admin
  admin
  warehouse_staff
  purchasing_staff
}

enum TenantStatus {
  active
  suspended
  trial
}

enum POStatus {
  draft
  ordered
  partially_received
  received
  cancelled
}

enum SerialNumberStatus {
  in_stock
  issued
  adjusted
}

enum MovementType {
  stock_in
  stock_out
  adjustment
}

enum NotificationStatus {
  sent
  failed
  retried
}

enum AdjustmentReason {
  recount
  damage
  theft
  correction
  other
}

// ─── Platform-level models ───────────────────────────────────

model Tenant {
  id           String       @id @default(cuid())
  name         String
  slug         String       @unique
  contactEmail String       @map("contact_email")
  status       TenantStatus @default(active)
  createdAt    DateTime     @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt    DateTime     @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  users                     User[]
  products                  Product[]
  suppliers                 Supplier[]
  purchaseOrders            PurchaseOrder[]
  purchaseOrderItems        PurchaseOrderItem[]
  stockIns                  StockIn[]
  stockInItems              StockInItem[]
  stockOuts                 StockOut[]
  stockOutItems             StockOutItem[]
  serialNumbers             SerialNumber[]
  stockAdjustments          StockAdjustment[]
  stockAdjustmentItems      StockAdjustmentItem[]
  stockMovementLogs         StockMovementLog[]
  auditLogs                 AuditLog[]
  lowStockNotificationLogs  LowStockNotificationLog[]

  @@map("tenants")
}

model User {
  id             String    @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId       String?   @map("tenant_id")
  tenant         Tenant?   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  name           String
  email          String    @unique
  hashedPassword String    @map("hashed_password")
  role           UserRole
  isActive       Boolean   @default(true) @map("is_active")
  lastLoginAt    DateTime? @map("last_login_at")
  createdAt      DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime  @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  createdPurchaseOrders  PurchaseOrder[]       @relation("po_created_by")
  receivedStockIns       StockIn[]             @relation("stock_in_received_by")
  releasedStockOuts      StockOut[]            @relation("stock_out_released_by")
  createdAdjustments     StockAdjustment[]     @relation("adjustment_created_by")
  approvedAdjustments    StockAdjustment[]     @relation("adjustment_approved_by")
  performedMovements     StockMovementLog[]    @relation("movement_performed_by")
  actedAuditLogs         AuditLog[]            @relation("audit_actor")
  lowStockNotifications  LowStockNotificationLog[]

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([email])
  @@map("users")
}

model VerificationToken {
  identifier String
  token      String   @unique
  expires    DateTime

  @@id([identifier, token])
  @@map("verification_tokens")
}

// ─── Tenant-scoped models ────────────────────────────────────

model Product {
  id                    String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId              String   @map("tenant_id")
  tenant                Tenant   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  productCode           String   @map("product_code")
  barcodeValue          String   @map("barcode_value")
  name                  String
  category              String
  unit                  String
  supplierCost          Decimal  @map("supplier_cost") @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  markupPercent         Decimal  @map("markup_percent") @db.Decimal(8, 2)
  sellingPrice          Decimal  @map("selling_price") @db.Decimal(12, 2)
  currentQuantity       Int      @default(0) @map("current_quantity")
  lowStockThreshold     Int      @map("low_stock_threshold")
  serialTrackingEnabled Boolean  @default(false) @map("serial_tracking_enabled")
  isActive              Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
  createdAt             DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt             DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  purchaseOrderItems        PurchaseOrderItem[]
  stockInItems              StockInItem[]
  stockOutItems             StockOutItem[]
  serialNumbers             SerialNumber[]
  stockAdjustmentItems      StockAdjustmentItem[]
  stockMovementLogs         StockMovementLog[]
  lowStockNotificationLogs  LowStockNotificationLog[]

  @@unique([tenantId, productCode])
  @@unique([tenantId, barcodeValue])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([tenantId, category])
  @@map("products")
}

model Supplier {
  id            String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId      String   @map("tenant_id")
  tenant        Tenant   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  name          String
  contactPerson String?  @map("contact_person")
  phone         String?
  email         String?
  address       String?
  notes         String?
  isActive      Boolean  @default(true) @map("is_active")
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt     DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  purchaseOrders PurchaseOrder[]

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("suppliers")
}

model PurchaseOrder {
  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId        String   @map("tenant_id")
  tenant          Tenant   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  poNumber        String   @map("po_number")
  supplierId      String   @map("supplier_id")
  supplier        Supplier @relation(fields: [supplierId], references: [id])
  orderDate       DateTime @map("order_date")
  expectedDate    DateTime? @map("expected_date")
  status          POStatus @default(draft)
  notes           String?
  attachmentUrl   String?  @map("attachment_url")
  createdByUserId String   @map("created_by_user_id")
  createdByUser   User     @relation("po_created_by", fields: [createdByUserId], references: [id])
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt       DateTime @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  items    PurchaseOrderItem[]
  stockIns StockIn[]

  @@unique([tenantId, poNumber])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([supplierId])
  @@index([tenantId, status])
  @@map("purchase_orders")
}

model PurchaseOrderItem {
  id                   String  @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId             String  @map("tenant_id")
  tenant               Tenant  @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  purchaseOrderId      String  @map("purchase_order_id")
  purchaseOrder        PurchaseOrder @relation(fields: [purchaseOrderId], references: [id])
  productId            String  @map("product_id")
  product              Product @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  orderedQty           Int     @map("ordered_qty")
  receivedQty          Int     @default(0) @map("received_qty")
  supplierCostSnapshot Decimal @map("supplier_cost_snapshot") @db.Decimal(12, 2)

  @@index([purchaseOrderId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("purchase_order_items")
}

model StockIn {
  id              String    @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId        String    @map("tenant_id")
  tenant          Tenant    @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  referenceNumber String    @map("reference_number")
  purchaseOrderId String?   @map("purchase_order_id")
  purchaseOrder   PurchaseOrder? @relation(fields: [purchaseOrderId], references: [id])
  receivedDate    DateTime  @map("received_date")
  receivedByUserId String   @map("received_by_user_id")
  receivedByUser  User      @relation("stock_in_received_by", fields: [receivedByUserId], references: [id])
  notes           String?
  attachmentUrl   String?   @map("attachment_url")
  createdAt       DateTime  @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  items StockInItem[]

  @@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_ins")
}

model StockInItem {
  id                   String  @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId             String  @map("tenant_id")
  tenant               Tenant  @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  stockInId            String  @map("stock_in_id")
  stockIn              StockIn @relation(fields: [stockInId], references: [id])
  productId            String  @map("product_id")
  product              Product @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  quantity             Int
  supplierCostSnapshot Decimal @map("supplier_cost_snapshot") @db.Decimal(12, 2)

  serialNumbers SerialNumber[]

  @@index([stockInId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_in_items")
}

model StockOut {
  id                 String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId           String   @map("tenant_id")
  tenant             Tenant   @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  referenceNumber    String   @map("reference_number")
  releasedDate       DateTime @map("released_date")
  releasedByUserId   String   @map("released_by_user_id")
  releasedByUser     User     @relation("stock_out_released_by", fields: [releasedByUserId], references: [id])
  requestedByName    String   @map("requested_by_name")
  usedFor            String   @map("used_for")
  notes              String?
  printableSlipNumber String  @map("printable_slip_number")
  createdAt          DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  items StockOutItem[]

  @@unique([tenantId, referenceNumber])
  @@unique([tenantId, printableSlipNumber])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_outs")
}

model StockOutItem {
  id                   String  @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId             String  @map("tenant_id")
  tenant               Tenant  @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  stockOutId           String  @map("stock_out_id")
  stockOut             StockOut @relation(fields: [stockOutId], references: [id])
  productId            String  @map("product_id")
  product              Product @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  quantity             Int
  sellingPriceSnapshot Decimal @map("selling_price_snapshot") @db.Decimal(12, 2)

  serialNumbers SerialNumber[]

  @@index([stockOutId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_out_items")
}

model SerialNumber {
  id             String             @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId       String             @map("tenant_id")
  tenant         Tenant             @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  productId      String             @map("product_id")
  product        Product            @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  serialValue    String             @map("serial_value")
  barcodeValue   String?            @map("barcode_value")
  status         SerialNumberStatus @default(in_stock)
  stockInItemId  String?            @map("stock_in_item_id")
  stockInItem    StockInItem?       @relation(fields: [stockInItemId], references: [id])
  stockOutItemId String?            @map("stock_out_item_id")
  stockOutItem   StockOutItem?      @relation(fields: [stockOutItemId], references: [id])
  createdAt      DateTime           @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  updatedAt      DateTime           @updatedAt @map("updated_at")

  stockAdjustmentItems StockAdjustmentItem[]
  stockMovementLogs    StockMovementLog[]

  @@unique([tenantId, productId, serialValue])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([status])
  @@map("serial_numbers")
}

model StockAdjustment {
  id               String           @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId         String           @map("tenant_id")
  tenant           Tenant           @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  adjustmentDate   DateTime         @map("adjustment_date")
  reason           AdjustmentReason
  notes            String?
  createdByUserId  String           @map("created_by_user_id")
  createdByUser    User             @relation("adjustment_created_by", fields: [createdByUserId], references: [id])
  approvedByUserId String?          @map("approved_by_user_id")
  approvedByUser   User?            @relation("adjustment_approved_by", fields: [approvedByUserId], references: [id])
  createdAt        DateTime         @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  items StockAdjustmentItem[]

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_adjustments")
}

model StockAdjustmentItem {
  id                String           @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId          String           @map("tenant_id")
  tenant            Tenant           @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  stockAdjustmentId String           @map("stock_adjustment_id")
  stockAdjustment   StockAdjustment  @relation(fields: [stockAdjustmentId], references: [id])
  productId         String           @map("product_id")
  product           Product          @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  quantityDelta     Int              @map("quantity_delta")
  serialNumberId    String?          @map("serial_number_id")
  serialNumber      SerialNumber?    @relation(fields: [serialNumberId], references: [id])

  @@index([stockAdjustmentId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([tenantId])
  @@map("stock_adjustment_items")
}

model StockMovementLog {
  id                String       @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId          String       @map("tenant_id")
  tenant            Tenant       @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  productId         String       @map("product_id")
  product           Product      @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  movementType      MovementType @map("movement_type")
  referenceType     String       @map("reference_type")
  referenceId       String       @map("reference_id")
  quantityBefore    Int          @map("quantity_before")
  quantityDelta     Int          @map("quantity_delta")
  quantityAfter     Int          @map("quantity_after")
  serialNumberId    String?      @map("serial_number_id")
  serialNumber      SerialNumber? @relation(fields: [serialNumberId], references: [id])
  performedByUserId String       @map("performed_by_user_id")
  performedByUser   User         @relation("movement_performed_by", fields: [performedByUserId], references: [id])
  requestedByName   String?      @map("requested_by_name")
  usedFor           String?      @map("used_for")
  performedAt       DateTime     @map("performed_at")
  notes             String?

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@index([tenantId, referenceType, referenceId])
  @@map("stock_movement_logs")
}

model AuditLog {
  id               String   @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId         String?  @map("tenant_id")
  tenant           Tenant?  @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  actorUserId      String   @map("actor_user_id")
  actorUser        User     @relation("audit_actor", fields: [actorUserId], references: [id])
  actionType       String   @map("action_type")
  entityType       String   @map("entity_type")
  entityId         String   @map("entity_id")
  fieldChangesJson Json?    @map("field_changes_json")
  beforeStateJson  Json?    @map("before_state_json")
  afterStateJson   Json?    @map("after_state_json")
  createdAt        DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([actorUserId])
  @@index([entityType, entityId])
  @@index([createdAt])
  @@map("audit_logs")
}

model LowStockNotificationLog {
  id               String             @id @default(cuid())
  tenantId         String             @map("tenant_id")
  tenant           Tenant             @relation(fields: [tenantId], references: [id])
  productId        String             @map("product_id")
  product          Product            @relation(fields: [productId], references: [id])
  notifiedToUserId String             @map("notified_to_user_id")
  notifiedToUser   User               @relation(fields: [notifiedToUserId], references: [id])
  sentAt           DateTime           @map("sent_at")
  status           NotificationStatus

  @@index([tenantId])
  @@index([productId])
  @@map("low_stock_notification_logs")
}

```
